metadata.title: Renal-Safe Recipe Database — USDA-Verified Nutrition
metadata.description: 252 kidney-friendly recipes across Western, Chinese, and Thai cuisines. All nutritional values are PER SERVING, calculated from USDA FoodData Central ingredient data.
metadata.disclaimer: MEDICAL DISCLAIMER: All nutritional values are approximate, sourced from USDA FoodData Central. Always consult your nephrologist or renal dietitian. Potassium and phosphorus limits vary by CKD stage and individual lab values.
metadata.nutrition_standard: PER SERVING — All values are per single serving. Divide whole-dish ingredients by the 'servings' field to determine individual portion.
metadata.ingredient_match_rate: 100.0% of ingredients matched to USDA FoodData Central database

recipes.name: Steamed White Fish with Green Onions
recipes.description: Delicate white fish steamed with fresh green onions and ginger
instructions: Rinse fish and pat dry. Place on a heatproof plate.
Slice ginger and garlic thinly, spread over fish.
Add carrot and zucchini slices around fish.
Steam over boiling water for 8–10 minutes until just cooked.
Drizzle soy sauce and sesame oil.
Heat canola oil until hot and pour over fish to release aroma.
Garnish with spring onion and serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 335.2mg, Na 83.0mg, Phos 114.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Cabbage with Garlic
recipes.description: Crispy cabbage lightly stir-fried with garlic and sesame oil
instructions: Heat canola oil in a wok over medium heat.
Add minced garlic and ginger, stir until fragrant.
Add napa cabbage and stir-fry on high heat.
Cook until slightly softened but still crunchy.
Add rice vinegar and sesame oil.
Toss well and serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 334.3mg, Na 12.7mg, Phos 45.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg Fried Rice with Carrots
recipes.description: Light and fluffy fried rice made with eggs and vegetables
instructions: Heat oil in a wok, add garlic and sauté.
Add egg whites and scramble lightly.
Add cooked rice and break up clumps.
Add carrots, peas, and spring onion.
Stir-fry evenly, drizzle sesame oil.
Toss until heated through and slightly dry.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 338.1mg, Na 138.6mg, Phos 111.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Ginger Chicken Soup
recipes.description: Warming broth with tender chicken and fresh ginger
instructions: Bring water to boil, add sliced ginger.
Add chicken breast and simmer gently.
Skim impurities for a clear broth.
Add cabbage and carrots, cook 10–15 minutes.
Add rice wine and sesame oil.
Garnish with spring onion before serving.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 375.5mg, Na 71.9mg, Phos 170.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Bok Choy with Garlic
recipes.description: Quick Cantonese-style bok choy stir-fried with garlic, sesame oil, and a touch of low-sodium soy sauce.
instructions: Heat oil in wok.
Add garlic, sauté until fragrant.
Add bok choy, stir-fry on high heat.
Add soy sauce and sesame oil.
Cook until just tender and bright green.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 282.4mg, Na 113.7mg, Phos 49.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Cashew Chicken Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Tender chicken pieces with crunchy cashews and vegetables
instructions: Slice chicken thinly and coat lightly with cornstarch.
Heat oil, stir-fry chicken until almost cooked.
Add garlic and ginger.
Add vegetables and stir-fry.
Add cashews and rice wine.
Finish with sesame oil and toss well.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 549.3mg, Na 77.6mg, Phos 295.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Cucumber Salad with Rice Vinegar
recipes.description: Refreshing cold cucumber salad with tangy rice vinegar dressing
instructions: Slice cucumber thinly into rounds. Mince garlic finely.
Whisk together minced garlic, rice vinegar, honey, and sesame oil until honey dissolves.
Toss cucumber in dressing. Add fresh coriander and toss again.
Refrigerate 15 minutes before serving so the dressing penetrates the cucumber.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 171.6mg, Na 3.9mg, Phos 29.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Pork with Preserved Vegetables
recipes.description: Tender steamed pork with traditional fermented vegetables
instructions: Mix ground pork with garlic, ginger, rice wine, honey, and cornstarch.
Place in a shallow dish.
Steam for 15–20 minutes until cooked through.
Drizzle sesame oil before serving.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 428.9mg, Na 84.7mg, Phos 232.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Broccoli with Garlic
recipes.description: Fresh broccoli florets steamed and topped with garlic
instructions: Break broccoli into evenly sized florets. Blanch in boiling water 2–3 minutes until bright green and tender-crisp. Drain immediately.
Heat canola oil and sesame oil together in a small pan. Add minced garlic and cook 30 seconds until just golden.
Add low-sodium soy sauce to the garlic oil and stir.
Pour the garlic oil and soy sauce over the broccoli. Toss to coat evenly. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 350.4mg, Na 81.8mg, Phos 79.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Clear Mushroom Soup
recipes.description: Light broth with fresh mushrooms and ginger
instructions: Soak mushrooms and fungus if dried.
Bring water to boil with ginger.
Add mushrooms, tofu, and carrot.
Simmer 10–15 minutes.
Add rice wine and sesame oil.
Thicken slightly with cornstarch slurry.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 157.8mg, Na 32.7mg, Phos 51.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Rice Noodles
recipes.description: Soft rice noodles with vegetables and light sauce
instructions: Heat canola oil in a wok over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and stir until fragrant.
Add sliced chicken and cook until nearly done.
Add bean sprouts and chives, toss quickly.
Add cooked rice noodles and loosen gently.
Pour in soy sauce and rice wine.
Stir-fry on high heat until noodles absorb flavor.
Finish with sesame oil and serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 288.9mg, Na 231.1mg, Phos 199.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Poached Chicken with Corn
recipes.description: Tender chicken poached with sweet corn
instructions: Bring water to a gentle boil with ginger.
Add chicken breast and poach on low heat.
Add corn kernels and simmer 10–15 minutes.
Add rice wine and sesame oil.
Garnish with spring onion before serving.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 412.7mg, Na 83.6mg, Phos 261.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Tofu with Soy Sauce
recipes.description: Silken tofu steamed with aromatic soy sauce
instructions: Place silken tofu on a heatproof plate.
Scatter garlic, ginger, and spring onion over tofu.
Steam for 5–8 minutes until heated through.
Drizzle soy sauce, then hot oil.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 282.6mg, Na 129.7mg, Phos 163.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Carrot and Onion Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Sweet carrots and onions in light garlic sauce
instructions: Heat oil in wok.
Add garlic and ginger, stir until fragrant.
Add onions first and cook until translucent.
Add carrots and stir-fry until tender.
Add vinegar and honey.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 347.0mg, Na 55.2mg, Phos 48.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg Drop Soup
recipes.description: Light broth with silky egg ribbons
instructions: Bring water to boil with ginger.
Add cornstarch slurry to slightly thicken.
Slowly pour egg white while stirring to create ribbons.
Add rice wine and sesame oil.
Garnish with spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 78.0mg, Na 52.0mg, Phos 8.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed White Fish with Mushrooms
recipes.description: Delicate fish with fresh mushrooms
instructions: Place fish on plate, top with mushrooms, ginger, and spring onion.
Steam for 8–10 minutes.
Add soy sauce and rice wine.
Pour hot oil over fish.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 557.8mg, Na 141.8mg, Phos 239.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Green Beans
recipes.description: Tender green beans with garlic and ginger
instructions: Heat oil in wok.
Add garlic and ginger.
Add green beans and stir-fry on high heat.
Cook until slightly blistered.
Add vinegar and honey.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 249.1mg, Na 7.6mg, Phos 47.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Jasmine Rice Pilaf
recipes.description: Fragrant jasmine rice with vegetables
instructions: Heat oil in pot, sauté shallot, garlic, and ginger.
Add rice and stir to coat grains.
Add water and bring to boil.
Cover and simmer until cooked.
Fluff and garnish with parsley.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 142.1mg, Na 6.9mg, Phos 87.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Shrimp and Vegetable Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Quick shrimp stir-fry with mixed vegetables
instructions: Heat oil in wok.
Add garlic and ginger.
Add shrimp and cook until pink.
Add vegetables and stir-fry.
Add rice wine and cornstarch slurry.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 473.2mg, Na 93.7mg, Phos 219.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Chicken with Ginger
recipes.description: Moist chicken steamed with aromatic ginger
instructions: Place chicken breast in a heatproof shallow dish. Scatter ginger slices and spring onion over and around the chicken.
Pour rice wine and water over the chicken.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place dish in steamer. Cover tightly.
Steam over medium-high heat 18–20 minutes until chicken is fully cooked — pierce the thickest part and juices should run completely clear.
Drizzle sesame oil over the chicken. Serve immediately with steamed white rice, spooning the steaming juices over.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 321.9mg, Na 78.4mg, Phos 227.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Braised Tofu with Vegetables
recipes.description: Soft tofu braised with fresh vegetables
instructions: Cut tofu into cubes and lightly pat dry.
Heat oil, sauté garlic and ginger.
Add tofu and sear lightly on all sides.
Add vegetables and stir-fry briefly.
Add soy sauce and rice wine.
Add small amount of water and simmer.
Thicken with cornstarch slurry.
Finish with sesame oil and spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 416.1mg, Na 94.4mg, Phos 239.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Fish with Black Beans
recipes.description: White fish steamed with fermented black beans
instructions: Place fish on plate with garlic, ginger, and chili.
Steam for 8–10 minutes.
Drizzle rice wine.
Heat oil and pour over fish.
Finish with sesame oil and spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 455.9mg, Na 74.7mg, Phos 209.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken Rice Soup
recipes.description: Light rice soup with shredded chicken
instructions: Boil water with ginger.
Add chicken and simmer.
Add rice and cook until grains soften.
Add vegetables and cook until tender.
Add sesame oil and rice wine.
Garnish with spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 334.8mg, Na 72.4mg, Phos 181.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Pumpkin
recipes.description: Sweet pumpkin stir-fried with garlic
instructions: Heat oil and sauté garlic and ginger.
Add pumpkin slices.
Stir-fry until slightly soft.
Add rice wine and honey.
Cook until tender.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 330.2mg, Na 6.4mg, Phos 51.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Shrimp Dumplings
recipes.description: Light shrimp dumplings steamed
instructions: Mix shrimp with chopped water chestnuts, ginger, and seasoning.
Add cornstarch to bind.
Wrap in wheat starch dough.
Steam for 6–8 minutes until translucent.
Brush with sesame oil before serving.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 223.5mg, Na 60.3mg, Phos 132.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Clear Broth with Vegetables
recipes.description: Simple clear broth with seasonal vegetables
instructions: Peel and cut daikon radish and carrot into 3 cm pieces. Slice zucchini and napa cabbage.
Bring 900 ml water to a boil in a medium pot with sliced ginger.
Add all vegetables — daikon, carrot, zucchini, and napa cabbage. Reduce to a gentle simmer.
Simmer uncovered 20–25 minutes until daikon is completely tender and translucent.
Remove and discard ginger. Drizzle sesame oil. Garnish with sliced spring onion. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 362.5mg, Na 30.8mg, Phos 42.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Kung Pao Chicken
recipes.description: Spicy chicken with peanuts (portion controlled)
instructions: Dice chicken and coat lightly with cornstarch.
Heat oil, stir-fry chicken until cooked.
Add garlic and ginger.
Add vegetables and peanuts.
Add vinegar, honey, and rice wine.
Toss quickly and finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 508.8mg, Na 77.3mg, Phos 268.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Bean Sprouts
recipes.description: Quick bean sprouts in garlic sauce
instructions: Heat canola oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Add minced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add bean sprouts all at once. Toss vigorously over maximum heat for 1.5–2 minutes — they should stay crunchy.
Add rice vinegar and toss 20 seconds. Remove from heat.
Drizzle sesame oil. Scatter sliced spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 233.4mg, Na 9.8mg, Phos 77.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Egg
recipes.description: Silky steamed egg custard
instructions: Whisk egg whites with 200 ml cooled boiled water and white pepper in broad, slow strokes — do not incorporate air bubbles.
Strain the mixture twice through a fine-mesh sieve into a heatproof bowl for a perfectly smooth surface.
Skim any surface foam with a spoon.
Cover the bowl tightly with a small plate or foil. Steam over barely simmering water on low heat for 10–12 minutes until set.
Drizzle sesame oil. Scatter sliced spring onion. Serve in the bowl immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 111.6mg, Na 100.4mg, Phos 10.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: White Radish Soup
recipes.description: Healing radish soup with ginger
instructions: Blanch ground pork in boiling water 2 minutes, drain, and rinse under cold running water to remove impurities.
In a fresh pot, bring 900 ml water to a boil with sliced ginger. Add blanched pork.
Add diced daikon radish and carrot rounds. Add rice wine. Reduce to a low simmer. Cover partially.
Simmer 45–50 minutes until daikon is completely tender, translucent, and has absorbed the broth.
Season with white pepper only. Drizzle sesame oil. Garnish with spring onion. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 460.8mg, Na 66.9mg, Phos 120.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken with Mushrooms
recipes.description: Tender chicken with fresh mushrooms
instructions: Slice chicken and coat lightly with cornstarch.
Heat oil, sauté garlic and ginger.
Add chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Add mushrooms and fungus.
Add rice wine and stir-fry.
Add small water, simmer briefly.
Finish with sesame oil and spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 326.9mg, Na 86.3mg, Phos 201.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Leek
recipes.description: Fresh leeks in light garlic sauce
instructions: Trim root and dark green tops from leek. Slice the white and pale green parts into 2 cm rounds. Rinse thoroughly between layers.
Heat canola oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry 20 seconds.
Add leek rounds. Stir-fry 3–4 minutes over medium-high heat until softened and lightly caramelised at the edges.
Add rice wine, toss 30 seconds.
Remove from heat. Drizzle sesame oil. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 208.9mg, Na 22.3mg, Phos 42.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Seafood Congee
recipes.description: Rice porridge with seafood and ginger
instructions: Rinse rice and boil with water.
Add ginger and simmer until porridge forms.
Add shrimp and fish.
Cook until seafood is done.
Add rice wine and sesame oil.
Garnish with spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 225.1mg, Na 48.2mg, Phos 145.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Bok Choy with Honey Soy Glaze
recipes.description: Blanched bok choy in a light honey and low-sodium soy glaze thickened with cornstarch — a kidney-safe take on the classic oyster sauce version.
instructions: Heat oil and sauté garlic.
Add bok choy and stir-fry.
Mix soy sauce, honey, rice wine, and cornstarch.
Pour sauce in and cook until thickened.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 285.8mg, Na 115.0mg, Phos 50.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Braised Mushroom Soup
recipes.description: Rich mushroom broth with vegetables
instructions: Soak dried shiitake mushrooms 30 minutes in cold water until softened. Squeeze out moisture. Discard stems, slice caps.
In a pot, bring 700 ml water to a boil with sliced ginger and rice wine.
Add sliced chicken breast and shiitake mushroom caps. Skim any foam. Add red dates.
Reduce to a low simmer. Cover and cook 20–25 minutes.
Add wolfberries in the last 5 minutes. Remove ginger. Drizzle sesame oil. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 176.6mg, Na 35.4mg, Phos 90.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg Noodle Soup
recipes.description: Light broth with soft egg noodles
instructions: Cook rice noodles in unsalted boiling water until tender. Drain and rinse under cold water.
Poach chicken breast in low-sodium broth with ginger slices over gentle heat 18 minutes. Remove and slice thinly.
Add sliced carrot to the simmering broth. Cook 5 minutes. Add bok choy and cook 2 more minutes.
Divide noodles between two bowls. Arrange sliced chicken and vegetables on top.
Ladle hot broth over everything. Drizzle sesame oil. Garnish with spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 664.2mg, Na 605.1mg, Phos 235.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Pork Ribs
recipes.description: Tender ribs steamed with black beans
instructions: Combine pork ribs with ginger slices, spring onion, rice wine, and cornstarch in a bowl. Toss to coat and marinate 15 minutes.
Arrange ribs in a single layer on a shallow heatproof plate. Pour any marinade over.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly.
Steam over medium-high heat 22–25 minutes until ribs are cooked through.
Drain excess liquid. Drizzle sesame oil. Scatter spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 313.0mg, Na 71.8mg, Phos 171.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Carrot Egg Fried Rice
recipes.description: Light fried rice with carrots and egg
instructions: Shred carrot finely. Slice spring onion. Use cold day-old rice for the best texture.
Heat canola oil in a wok over high heat. Add minced garlic, stir-fry 15 seconds.
Add egg whites. Scramble in slow strokes until just set. Remove to a plate.
Add rice to the wok. Press against the hot wok surface 1 minute without stirring to develop a slight crust. Then toss.
Add shredded carrot and stir-fry 2 minutes. Return egg whites. Add spring onion. Toss.
Drizzle sesame oil. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 291.5mg, Na 105.8mg, Phos 92.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Vegetable Tofu Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Silken tofu with mixed vegetables
instructions: Heat oil, sauté garlic and ginger.
Add tofu and sear lightly.
Add vegetables and stir-fry.
Add soy sauce and rice wine.
Thicken with cornstarch slurry.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 446.3mg, Na 71.5mg, Phos 246.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed White Fish with Peppers
recipes.description: Fish with colorful bell peppers
instructions: Pat fish fillet dry. Score the skin with 2–3 shallow cuts. Place on a heatproof plate.
Scatter sliced ginger over the fish. Arrange sliced red and yellow bell peppers around and on top.
Add spring onion pieces. Drizzle rice wine over everything.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly.
Steam over medium-high heat 10–12 minutes until fish is opaque throughout.
Heat canola oil in a small saucepan until smoking. Pour over the fish and peppers — it sizzles and blooms the aromatics.
Drizzle sesame oil. Serve immediately in the steaming plate.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 509.0mg, Na 74.6mg, Phos 211.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Congee with Minced Pork
recipes.description: Silky rice porridge simmered with seasoned minced pork and ginger — the classic Cantonese breakfast.
instructions: Rinse rice and cook with water until porridge forms.
Add ginger and simmer.
Mix pork with cornstarch and add to pot.
Stir to break meat evenly.
Add egg white slowly and stir.
Finish with sesame oil and spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 187.3mg, Na 42.4mg, Phos 96.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Silken Tofu with Ginger-Scallion Oil
recipes.description: Cold silken tofu dressed with a sizzling hot ginger-scallion oil — a no-cook protein dish.
instructions: Refrigerate silken tofu at least 1 hour before serving so it is very cold.
Combine grated ginger, sliced spring onion, and fresh coriander in a small heatproof bowl.
Carefully unmould the chilled tofu onto a shallow serving plate. Slice into 1.5 cm slabs without dragging the knife.
Scatter the ginger-spring onion-coriander mixture over the top.
Heat canola oil in a very small saucepan over high heat until it just begins to smoke. Immediately pour over the aromatics — it sizzles loudly and cooks them instantly.
Drizzle rice vinegar and sesame oil around the edges. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 279.0mg, Na 71.0mg, Phos 152.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Winter Melon and Chicken Soup
recipes.description: Traditional Cantonese slow-simmered tonic soup — winter melon is the most kidney-safe vegetable.
instructions: Peel winter melon, remove seeds, cut into 3 cm cubes.
Blanch chicken breast in boiling water 3 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold running water.
In a fresh pot, bring 900 ml water to a boil with ginger slices and rice wine. Add blanched chicken. Skim foam.
Add winter melon cubes. Reduce to a medium-low simmer. Cover and cook 30 minutes until melon is completely translucent and silky.
Add wolfberries in the last 5 minutes. Remove ginger slices. Drizzle sesame oil. Garnish with spring onion. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 295.2mg, Na 158.6mg, Phos 137.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Pork Patty with Ginger
recipes.description: A Cantonese home classic — steamed minced pork patty seasoned only with ginger and rice wine.
instructions: Combine ground pork with grated ginger, sliced spring onion, egg white, rice wine, cornstarch, and sesame oil in a bowl.
Mix vigorously in one direction for 2–3 minutes until the mixture becomes sticky and cohesive.
Transfer to a shallow rimmed heatproof plate. Wet your hand and smooth into an even patty about 1.5 cm thick.
Bring steamer water to a rolling boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly.
Steam over high heat 18–20 minutes. Cut into the very centre to verify — no pink should remain.
Drain any accumulated liquid. Drizzle sesame oil. Scatter fresh spring onion. Serve directly from the plate.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 450.2mg, Na 109.4mg, Phos 229.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Choy Sum with Garlic
recipes.description: Cantonese restaurant-style garlicky choy sum — blanched first to reduce potassium.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil. Blanch choy sum for 90 seconds. Drain. Plunge into ice water 1 minute. Drain thoroughly and pat dry — this removes potassium.
Heat canola oil in a wok over the highest heat for 2 minutes until smoking.
Add sliced garlic. Stir-fry 20 seconds until pale gold.
Add all blanched and dried choy sum at once. Toss vigorously over maximum heat 2 minutes until bright green.
Add rice wine around the wok rim. Toss 30 seconds. Remove from heat. Drizzle sesame oil. Plate and serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 372.1mg, Na 33.1mg, Phos 55.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pear and Snow Fungus Sweet Soup
recipes.description: Classic Cantonese sweet dessert soup — snow fungus has near-zero potassium and phosphorus.
instructions: Soak dried snow fungus 30 minutes in cold water until fully expanded and soft. Trim and remove the hard yellow base nub. Break into 4 cm pieces.
Peel and core Asian pear. Cut into 2.5 cm cubes.
Combine snow fungus pieces, pear cubes, pitted red dates, wolfberries, and 800 ml water in a pot.
Bring to a boil. Skim foam. Reduce to a low simmer. Cook uncovered 35–40 minutes until snow fungus is very tender and gelatinous.
Remove from heat. Stir in honey until dissolved. Serve warm, or cool and refrigerate to serve chilled.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 100.8mg, Na 7.8mg, Phos 10.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Bean Sprout and Carrot Salad
recipes.description: Cold sesame-dressed sprout salad — both vegetables are among the lowest-mineral choices.
instructions: Blanch bean sprouts in boiling water 30 seconds. Drain. Plunge into ice water 1 minute. Drain and pat dry.
Blanch carrot julienne in boiling water 1 minute. Plunge into ice water. Drain and pat dry.
Whisk together sesame oil, rice vinegar, honey, grated ginger, and white pepper until honey dissolves.
Combine cooled bean sprouts and carrot in a bowl. Pour dressing over. Toss well.
Add sliced spring onion and fresh coriander. Toss once more. Refrigerate 10 minutes before serving.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 246.4mg, Na 27.7mg, Phos 55.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Minced Pork with Water Chestnuts
recipes.description: Steamed pork patty with crunchy water chestnuts — classic dim sum-inspired home cooking.
instructions: Drain and rinse canned water chestnuts. Mince into fine 3–4 mm pieces.
Combine ground pork with minced water chestnuts, grated ginger, sliced spring onion, rice wine, sesame oil, and cornstarch.
Mix vigorously in one direction 2–3 minutes until sticky and cohesive.
Transfer to a shallow rimmed heatproof plate. Smooth into an even 1 cm thick patty.
Steam over high heat 18–20 minutes until centre is fully cooked with no pink. Drain accumulated liquid. Drizzle sesame oil. Serve.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 552.8mg, Na 81.7mg, Phos 222.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chrysanthemum Agar Jelly
recipes.description: Floral agar dessert brewed from chrysanthemum tea — near-zero mineral content.
instructions: Bring 500 ml water to a full boil. Remove from heat. Add chrysanthemum tea and steep 8 minutes. Strain out flowers.
Return strained tea to a clean saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a gentle boil.
While stirring constantly, sprinkle agar-agar powder in a slow stream. Whisk 2–3 minutes until completely dissolved.
Remove from heat. Stir in honey until dissolved.
Pour evenly into 4 moulds. Drop 2–3 wolfberries into each.
Cool at room temperature 30 minutes, then refrigerate at least 2 hours until firmly set. Unmould and serve chilled.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 46.2mg, Na 7.6mg, Phos 5.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Poached Chicken with Sesame Oil Sauce
recipes.description: White-cut chicken — the definitive Cantonese technique, dressed with hot sesame-ginger oil.
instructions: Set up an ice bath: fill a large bowl with cold water and add ice. Set beside the stove.
Place chicken breast in a saucepan with cold water and sliced ginger. Bring to a boil, then reduce immediately to the lowest simmer. Cover. Poach 18–20 minutes until juices run clear.
Transfer immediately to the ice bath. Submerge fully 5 minutes. Remove. Pat dry. Slice thinly or shred.
Place grated ginger and sliced spring onion in a small heatproof bowl. Heat sesame oil in a tiny pan until just smoking. Pour over the aromatics — it sizzles.
Arrange chicken on a plate. Spoon the sizzling sesame-ginger sauce over. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 318.5mg, Na 78.4mg, Phos 227.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Sea Bass with Lemon and Ginger
recipes.description: Premium Cantonese steamed sea bass — lemon replaces soy sauce for a sodium-free aromatic finish.
instructions: Pat sea bass fillets completely dry. Make 3 shallow diagonal cuts on the skin side to prevent curling.
Lay half the lemon slices on the steaming plate as a bed. Place sea bass on top.
Arrange remaining lemon slices and ginger julienne over the fish. Drizzle rice wine evenly.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly. Steam 10–12 minutes until opaque and flakes easily.
Remove from steamer. Drain all accumulated liquid. Scatter spring onion julienne over the fish.
Heat canola oil until smoking. Pour over spring onion — it sizzles. Drizzle sesame oil. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 424.8mg, Na 78.4mg, Phos 237.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Minced Chicken Lettuce Cups
recipes.description: Cantonese san choi bao — seasoned minced chicken in crisp iceberg cups, zero-sodium version.
instructions: Chill iceberg lettuce leaves in the refrigerator — cold cups hold the filling better.
Heat sesame oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Add minced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry 20 seconds.
Add ground chicken. Break into very fine pieces. Stir-fry continuously 5–6 minutes until cooked through.
Add minced water chestnuts. Stir-fry 1 minute. Add rice wine. Toss. Add sliced spring onion. Season with white pepper.
Taste — the filling should be savoury from sesame oil and rice wine without needing salt.
Spoon warm filling into chilled lettuce cups. Eat by hand immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 434.9mg, Na 93.6mg, Phos 206.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Clear Silken Tofu Soup
recipes.description: The simplest possible Chinese soup — silken tofu in clear broth, minimal mineral load.
instructions: Bring 500 ml water to a gentle simmer in a medium saucepan with ginger slices — do not boil vigorously.
Carefully cut silken tofu into 2 cm cubes directly in the packaging or on the plate, making clean downward cuts.
Lower tofu cubes gently into the simmering water using a wide ladle. Maintain a very gentle simmer.
Simmer 3 minutes — just enough to heat through. Do not stir.
Remove ginger. Drizzle sesame oil into the pot. Season with white pepper.
Ladle carefully into bowls keeping tofu pieces intact. Scatter spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 179.6mg, Na 46.4mg, Phos 100.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Chinese Broccoli with Garlic
recipes.description: Cantonese restaurant classic — gai lan blanched first to reduce potassium, then flash-fried with garlic.
instructions: Wash gai lan. Trim the bottom 2 cm of stems. Split any stems thicker than 1 cm lengthwise.
Blanch in boiling water 2 minutes. Drain. Transfer to ice water 1 minute. Drain thoroughly and pat dry.
Heat wok over the highest heat 2 full minutes until smoking. Add canola oil, swirl.
Add sliced garlic. Stir-fry 20 seconds until pale gold — move constantly to prevent burning.
Add all blanched gai lan. Toss vigorously 2 minutes over maximum heat. Add rice wine around the wok rim. Toss 30 more seconds.
Remove from heat. Drizzle sesame oil. Transfer to plate immediately. Serve.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 296.1mg, Na 25.0mg, Phos 54.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Rice Noodle Soup with Poached Chicken
recipes.description: Silky flat rice noodles in homemade clear broth with poached chicken — the quintessential light lunch.
instructions: Cook rice noodles in unsalted boiling water until just tender. Drain. Rinse under cold water. Divide between two deep bowls.
Poach chicken breast in low-sodium broth with ginger slices at a gentle simmer 18 minutes until cooked. Remove and slice thinly.
Blanch bok choy in fresh boiling water 1.5 minutes. Drain. Place beside noodles in each bowl.
Arrange sliced chicken over the noodles.
Return broth to a boil. Remove and discard ginger. Ladle hot broth over each bowl — enough to just submerge the noodles.
Drizzle sesame oil. Season with white pepper. Scatter spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 635.0mg, Na 601.8mg, Phos 252.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Pork with Wood Ear Mushrooms
recipes.description: Lean pork tenderloin with wood ear mushrooms — wood ear has near-zero potassium and phosphorus.
instructions: Slice pork and coat lightly with cornstarch.
Stir-fry garlic and ginger.
Add pork and cook.
Add mushrooms and spring onion.
Add rice wine.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 390.8mg, Na 68.2mg, Phos 184.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Tofu and Pumpkin Congee
recipes.description: Golden congee with kabocha pumpkin and silken tofu — naturally sweet and filling.
instructions: Wash rice. Combine rice, 1000 ml water, and diced kabocha pumpkin in a large pot.
Bring to a full boil over high heat. Stir once. Reduce to the very lowest simmer.
Cook uncovered 40 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes. The pumpkin will break apart and colour the congee golden.
Add silken tofu cubes very gently — place with a spoon and stir only once to avoid breaking them.
Add ginger julienne. Simmer 3 more minutes. Stir in honey. Season with white pepper.
Drizzle sesame oil. Ladle into bowls ensuring each serving gets tofu. Garnish with spring onion. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 206.0mg, Na 16.3mg, Phos 82.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Eggplant with Garlic Ginger Glaze
recipes.description: Yu xiang-inspired eggplant stir-fry without soy sauce — garlic, ginger, and rice vinegar provide all the flavour.
instructions: Heat oil and fry eggplant until soft.
Remove excess oil if needed.
Add garlic and ginger.
Add vinegar, rice wine, honey.
Toss eggplant in sauce.
Finish with sesame oil.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 271.6mg, Na 5.5mg, Phos 34.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Double-Boiled Snow Fungus with Asian Pear
recipes.description: Traditional medicinal tonic — double-boiling gently extracts collagen from snow fungus.
instructions: Soak snow fungus 30 minutes in cold water until fully expanded. Trim the hard yellow base. Break into 3–4 cm pieces.
Peel and core Asian pear. Cut into large chunks. Pit red dates.
Place snow fungus, pear, and dates in a heatproof ceramic casserole or bowl. Pour 700 ml water over.
Set up double boiler: place casserole inside a larger pot. Pour water into the outer pot halfway up the casserole sides. Cover the casserole tightly.
Bring outer water to a boil. Reduce to medium. Double-boil 50–60 minutes, topping up the outer water as needed.
Add wolfberries in the last 10 minutes. Remove lid. Stir in honey. Serve warm or refrigerate and serve chilled.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 106.4mg, Na 7.8mg, Phos 11.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Winter Melon and Prawn Clear Soup
recipes.description: Delicate clear soup with winter melon and prawns — one of the lowest-mineral combinations possible.
instructions: Peel winter melon, remove seeds, cut into 2 cm cubes. Peel and devein prawns.
Bring 600 ml water to a boil in a medium pot with ginger slices and rice wine.
Add winter melon cubes. Reduce to medium. Simmer 12–15 minutes until melon is slightly tender.
Add prawns. Simmer gently 2–3 minutes until they curl and turn fully pink. Do not overcook.
Season with white pepper. Drizzle sesame oil. Remove ginger. Garnish with spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 278.9mg, Na 170.4mg, Phos 143.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Braised Tofu with Shiitake Mushrooms
recipes.description: Home-style braised tofu in a light aromatic sauce — shiitake provides depth of flavour without salt.
instructions: Press firm tofu under a weighted plate 15 minutes. Cut into 2 cm cubes. Soak shiitake 30 minutes; slice caps.
Heat canola oil in a wide pan over medium-high. Add tofu in a single layer. Fry 2–3 minutes per side until golden. Remove.
In the same pan, add garlic and ginger. Stir-fry 20 seconds. Add sliced shiitake. Stir-fry 2 minutes.
Return tofu. Mix low-sodium soy sauce, rice wine, cornstarch, and 100 ml water into a sauce. Pour over everything.
Bring to a gentle simmer. Cook 3–4 minutes, turning tofu once, until sauce thickens to a glaze.
Drizzle sesame oil. Scatter spring onion. Season with white pepper. Serve over steamed white rice.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 246.5mg, Na 68.0mg, Phos 229.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Congee with Wolfberry and Ginger
recipes.description: Aromatic, restorative congee with goji berries and ginger — a traditional Chinese health breakfast.
instructions: Wash rice in 3–4 changes of cold water until clear. Combine with 850 ml water in a pot.
Bring to a full boil. Stir once. Reduce to the very lowest simmer.
Cook uncovered 40–45 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes with a wooden spoon.
Stir in wolfberries and ginger julienne 5 minutes before the end of cooking.
Remove from heat. Stir in honey. Season with white pepper. Drizzle sesame oil.
Ladle into bowls. Scatter spring onion. Serve piping hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 167.8mg, Na 22.2mg, Phos 61.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pork and Daikon Radish Soup
recipes.description: Cantonese slow-simmered clear pork and daikon soup — deeply nourishing, zero added sodium.
instructions: Blanch ground pork in boiling water 3 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water.
In a fresh pot, bring 900 ml water to a boil with ginger. Add blanched pork and rice wine. Skim foam.
Add daikon radish cubes and carrot rounds. Reduce to low. Cover partially. Simmer 50–60 minutes until daikon is tender.
Season with white pepper only. Drizzle sesame oil. Remove ginger. Garnish with spring onion. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 470.6mg, Na 73.7mg, Phos 139.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Green Beans and Tofu
recipes.description: Cantonese green bean and tofu stir-fry — no soy-based sauce, just sesame oil and rice wine.
instructions: Press firm tofu 15 minutes. Cut into 2 cm cubes. Pat dry.
Heat 8 ml canola oil in a wok. Pan-fry tofu on all sides until golden. Remove.
Blanch green beans in boiling water 2 minutes. Drain.
In the same wok, add remaining canola oil. Add minced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry 20 seconds.
Add blanched green beans. Stir-fry 2 minutes over high heat. Return tofu. Add rice wine. Toss 1 minute.
Drizzle sesame oil. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately over white rice.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 278.4mg, Na 17.5mg, Phos 178.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Lemongrass Fish Soup
recipes.description: Aromatic clear fish soup with lemongrass and ginger — clean, fragrant, and deeply savoury.
instructions: Bruise lemongrass stalk with the flat of a knife. Slice into rounds.
Bring 700 ml water to a boil with lemongrass and ginger slices. Simmer 8 minutes to build an aromatic broth.
Add daikon radish cubes. Simmer 8 minutes until slightly tender.
Add fish cubes. Reduce to a gentle simmer. Cook 6–8 minutes until fish is opaque. Do not boil vigorously or fish breaks apart.
Add rice wine. Season with white pepper. Drizzle sesame oil.
Remove and discard lemongrass and ginger. Garnish with spring onion. Ladle into bowls. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 365.5mg, Na 51.8mg, Phos 133.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Fish with Ginger and Spring Onion
recipes.description: The original Cantonese steamed fish — the simplest version, using only ginger, scallion, and hot oil.
instructions: Pat fish fillet completely dry. Score with 2–3 shallow diagonal cuts. Place on a heatproof plate.
Scatter ginger julienne and garlic slices over the fish. Add spring onion stalks. Drizzle rice wine.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly. Steam over medium-high heat 10–12 minutes.
Remove from steamer. Drain all accumulated liquid. Scatter spring onion julienne.
Heat canola oil in a small saucepan until smoking. Pour over spring onion — it sizzles and wilts immediately.
Drizzle sesame oil. Serve immediately in the steaming plate.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 457.8mg, Na 74.8mg, Phos 209.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Zucchini with Garlic
recipes.description: The quickest and most reliable Cantonese stir-fry — zucchini is consistently the lowest-mineral vegetable.
instructions: Cut zucchini into even 1.5 cm rounds. Slice garlic and cut ginger into fine julienne.
Heat wok over the highest heat 2 full minutes until it begins to smoke. Add canola oil. Swirl.
Add garlic and ginger. Toss continuously 20 seconds until garlic turns pale gold.
Add all zucchini at once. Spread in a single layer. Leave undisturbed 45 seconds. Then toss and stir-fry continuously 3 minutes.
Add rice wine around the wok rim. Toss 30 seconds. Remove from heat.
Drizzle sesame oil. Season with white pepper. Plate and serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 358.7mg, Na 12.2mg, Phos 56.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Congee with Sea Bass and Ginger
recipes.description: Premium Cantonese fish congee — fresh sea bass flakes cooked directly in silky rice porridge.
instructions: Wash rice. Combine with 900 ml cold water in a large pot. Bring to a full boil. Stir once. Reduce to the very lowest simmer.
Cook uncovered 40 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes.
Marinate sea bass fillet in rice wine 10 minutes. Remove any pin bones.
When congee is ready, increase heat to medium. Lower fish fillet in whole. Simmer gently 5–6 minutes until completely opaque.
Break fish apart gently into large flakes throughout the congee using two forks.
Stir in ginger julienne. Cook 1 more minute. Season with white pepper. Drizzle sesame oil.
Ladle into bowls. Scatter spring onion generously. Serve piping hot.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 271.7mg, Na 40.1mg, Phos 158.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Poached Chicken with Cucumber Sesame
recipes.description: Cold poached chicken served with julienned cucumber and a sesame-vinegar dressing.
instructions: Set up an ice bath beside the stove. Poach chicken breast in water with ginger slices at the lowest possible simmer 18–20 minutes until juices run clear.
Transfer immediately to ice bath. Submerge 5 minutes. Remove. Pat dry thoroughly. Slice thinly or shred.
Julienne cucumber into 4 cm strips. Place in a colander 10 minutes to drain excess water.
Whisk sesame oil, rice vinegar, honey, grated ginger, and white pepper until honey dissolves.
Arrange cucumber on a serving plate. Mound chicken on top. Pour dressing evenly over.
Garnish with spring onion. Serve immediately, cold.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 419.5mg, Na 77.9mg, Phos 243.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Mung Bean Glass Noodle Soup
recipes.description: Light, clear glass noodle soup — mung bean vermicelli has the lowest mineral content of all noodles.
instructions: Soak glass noodles in hot water 15 minutes. Drain. Cut into 15 cm lengths with scissors.
Bring 600 ml water or unsalted broth to a gentle simmer with ginger slices.
Add carrot rounds. Simmer 5 minutes. Add napa cabbage. Simmer 3 minutes.
Add soaked glass noodles. Simmer exactly 2 minutes — do not overcook or they become mushy.
Season with white pepper. Drizzle sesame oil. Remove ginger. Garnish with spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 202.1mg, Na 82.8mg, Phos 28.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Egg Custard with Prawns
recipes.description: Silky steamed egg white custard with two prawns on top — elegant protein presentation.
instructions: Marinate prawns in rice wine and white pepper 10 minutes.
Whisk egg whites with 150 ml cooled boiled water and white pepper in slow strokes. Strain through a fine sieve twice into 2 heatproof bowls.
Skim surface bubbles. Cover each bowl tightly with a small plate or foil.
Steam over barely simmering water on low heat 10 minutes. Gently place 2–3 marinated prawns on the partially-set surface of each bowl. Replace covers.
Steam 4 more minutes until custard is fully set and prawns are pink and cooked through.
Drizzle sesame oil. Scatter spring onion. Serve immediately in the steaming bowls.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 166.9mg, Na 109.8mg, Phos 80.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Asparagus with Ginger
recipes.description: Cantonese-style asparagus stir-fry — one of the lowest-potassium vegetables to go through the wok.
instructions: Snap or trim the woody bottom 3–4 cm from asparagus. Cut diagonally into 3 cm pieces.
Heat wok over the highest heat 2 minutes. Add canola oil. Swirl.
Add ginger julienne and garlic slices. Stir-fry moving constantly 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add asparagus. Spread in a single layer. Leave undisturbed 45 seconds. Then toss and stir-fry 3–4 minutes until tender-crisp with slight char on the tips.
Add rice wine around the wok rim. Toss 30 seconds. Remove from heat. Drizzle sesame oil. Plate immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 237.1mg, Na 4.5mg, Phos 60.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Snow Fungus and Wolfberry Sweet Soup
recipes.description: Nourishing cold tonic dessert with two of the most mineral-safe traditional Chinese ingredients.
instructions: Combine wolfberries, pitted red dates, and 700 ml water in a medium pot.
Bring to a boil over high heat. Skim foam. Reduce to a low simmer.
Simmer uncovered 30–35 minutes until dates are very soft and broth turns lightly golden.
Remove from heat. Stir in honey until fully dissolved.
Serve warm, or allow to cool and refrigerate to serve chilled.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 45.9mg, Na 9.3mg, Phos 6.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Braised Chicken with Red Dates and Ginger
recipes.description: Medicinal braised chicken — red dates and ginger create a naturally sweet, sodium-free braise.
instructions: Trim all visible fat from chicken thighs.
Heat sesame oil in a casserole or heavy-bottomed pan over medium-high. Add chicken thighs. Cook undisturbed 3 minutes per side until golden brown.
Remove chicken. Add ginger slices to the same pan. Stir-fry 30 seconds.
Return chicken. Add rice wine and let sizzle 30 seconds. Add pitted red dates and 200 ml water. Bring to a boil.
Reduce to low heat. Cover and braise 30–35 minutes until chicken is very tender. Remove lid and cook 3 minutes to reduce the sauce slightly.
Season with white pepper. Scatter spring onion. Serve over steamed white rice, spooning dates and sauce over.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 284.8mg, Na 78.6mg, Phos 182.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg White Foo Young (Steamed)
recipes.description: Steamed egg white patties with vegetables — lighter than the deep-fried restaurant version.
instructions: Blanch bean sprouts in boiling water 30 seconds. Blanch carrot julienne 1 minute. Plunge both into ice water. Drain. Pat dry.
Whisk egg whites with white pepper — just enough to break them up, not frothy.
Combine with blanched vegetables, sliced spring onion, and sesame oil.
Lightly oil 4 small heatproof ramekins. Pour egg white mixture evenly in.
Bring steamer water to a boil. Reduce to medium. Steam 12–15 minutes until centre is set — test with a gentle finger press.
Cool 2 minutes. Drizzle a little sesame oil before serving.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 226.1mg, Na 113.5mg, Phos 38.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken and Cabbage Dumplings (Steamed)
recipes.description: Homemade steamed dumplings — no-salt wrapper, chicken filling seasoned only with ginger and sesame oil.
instructions: Make dough: gradually pour 80 ml warm water into 150 g flour while stirring. Knead 5 minutes until smooth. Rest covered 20 minutes.
Salt-draw cabbage: toss shredded napa cabbage with a pinch of salt. Rest 10 minutes. Squeeze out all moisture. Rinse. Squeeze again until very dry.
Mix ground chicken with squeezed cabbage, grated ginger, spring onion, sesame oil, rice wine, and cornstarch. Stir vigorously in one direction 2 minutes.
Divide dough into 24 equal pieces. Roll each into an 8 cm circle. Fill with 1 teaspoon of filling. Fold and pleat edges to seal.
Steam on parchment-lined baskets over vigorously boiling water 12–15 minutes until wrappers are translucent.
Serve with rice vinegar dipping sauce.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 206.4mg, Na 39.6mg, Phos 120.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Lotus Leaf Sticky Rice
recipes.description: Fragrant glutinous rice steamed in an aromatic lotus leaf — festive but kidney-friendly when portioned.
instructions: Soak glutinous rice 2 hours. Drain very well. Soak lotus leaf in warm water 15 minutes. Pat dry.
Soak and slice shiitake mushrooms (discard stems). Dice cooked chicken breast.
Mix drained glutinous rice with diced chicken, sliced shiitake, grated ginger, spring onion, sesame oil, and rice wine.
Spread lotus leaf flat. Mound rice mixture in the centre, leaving 8 cm border. Fold sides over the rice, then top and bottom, to make a neat parcel.
Place parcel fold-side down on a steaming rack. Steam over high heat 40–45 minutes until rice is fully cooked and sticky.
Bring to the table whole. Unwrap dramatically to release fragrant steam. Scoop portions directly from the leaf.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 124.9mg, Na 24.0mg, Phos 82.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Sautéed Chinese Chives with Egg White
recipes.description: Cantonese chive stir-fry with silky egg white — quick, fragrant, and low-sodium.
instructions: Wash Chinese chives. Cut into 4 cm lengths, keeping thicker lower portions separate from leafy tips.
Whisk egg whites with white pepper in a small bowl.
Heat canola oil in a wok over high heat. Add egg whites and scramble in slow, broad strokes forming large soft curds. Remove while still slightly underdone.
Return wok to high heat. Add minced garlic. Stir-fry 15 seconds. Add chive stalks. Stir-fry 1 minute. Add chive leaves and tips. Stir-fry 1 more minute.
Return egg whites. Toss gently with chives 20 seconds. Drizzle sesame oil. Plate immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 334.9mg, Na 52.5mg, Phos 45.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Snow Peas with Ginger
recipes.description: Classic Cantonese snow pea stir-fry — flat snow peas are lower in potassium than regular peas.
instructions: String snow peas: snap the stem tip and pull the tough string along both edges. Rinse and dry thoroughly.
Heat wok over the highest heat 2 minutes. Add canola oil. Swirl.
Add sliced garlic and ginger julienne. Stir-fry moving constantly 20 seconds until just fragrant.
Add all snow peas. Spread in a single layer. Leave undisturbed 30 seconds. Then toss and stir-fry over high heat 2–3 minutes — they should remain bright green and distinctly crisp.
Add rice wine around the wok rim. Toss 20 seconds. Remove from heat immediately. Drizzle sesame oil. Season with white pepper. Plate and serve at once.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 234.6mg, Na 6.2mg, Phos 61.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Tofu and Cucumber Cold Salad
recipes.description: Chilled silken tofu and cucumber in sesame-ginger dressing — a refreshing no-cook summer dish.
instructions: Refrigerate silken tofu at least 30 minutes. Julienne cucumber into 4 cm strips. Place in a colander 10 minutes to drain.
Whisk sesame oil, rice vinegar, grated ginger, honey, and white pepper until honey dissolves.
Unmould chilled tofu onto a shallow serving plate. Slice into 1.5 cm slabs with clean, downward cuts.
Arrange cucumber julienne alongside the tofu slices.
Pour dressing evenly over both. Scatter sliced spring onion. Serve immediately, cold.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 295.0mg, Na 58.8mg, Phos 138.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Poached Fish in Ginger Broth
recipes.description: Minimalist Chinese poached fish — aromatics only, no seasoning salt.
instructions: Bring water to a gentle boil with ginger.
Add fish fillets and poach on low heat.
Add rice wine and simmer briefly.
Finish with sesame oil and spring onion.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 435.6mg, Na 74.0mg, Phos 202.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Stir-Fried Lotus Root with Vinegar
recipes.description: Suan liu ou pian — crisp vinegar-glazed lotus root slices, made sodium-free.
instructions: Peel lotus root. Slice into 3 mm rounds. Drop each slice immediately into cold water with a splash of rice vinegar to prevent discolouration.
Drain lotus root. Blanch in boiling water 2 minutes. Drain. Pat dry.
Whisk rice vinegar and honey until honey dissolves.
Heat wok over high heat. Add canola oil. Add minced garlic. Stir-fry 20 seconds until golden.
Add blanched lotus root. Toss and stir-fry 3 minutes over high heat until slightly more translucent.
Pour vinegar-honey glaze over. Toss continuously 1–2 minutes until sauce reduces to coat each slice. Remove from heat. Drizzle sesame oil. Plate.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 574.8mg, Na 41.1mg, Phos 106.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Plain Ginger Sesame Congee
recipes.description: The most minimal congee — ginger and sesame oil only. For acute phases and strictest restrictions.
instructions: Wash rice in 3–4 changes of cold water. Combine with 900 ml water in a large pot.
Bring to a full boil over high heat. Stir once. Reduce to the very lowest heat.
Cook uncovered 40–45 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes to prevent sticking.
Stir in fine ginger julienne 5 minutes before the end. Season with white pepper.
Remove from heat. Drizzle sesame oil generously. Stir gently.
Ladle into bowls. Scatter spring onion. Serve immediately — congee loses texture as it cools.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 108.2mg, Na 4.5mg, Phos 52.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Green Papaya and Pork Rib Soup
recipes.description: Classic Malaysian-Chinese tonic soup — green papaya is one of the lowest-potassium vegetables.
instructions: Blanch pork ribs: place in a pot, cover with cold water, bring to a full boil. Simmer 3 minutes. Drain. Rinse each rib under cold running water.
Peel green papaya. Scoop out seeds. Cut flesh into 3 cm cubes.
In a fresh large pot, bring 900 ml water to a boil with ginger, pitted red dates, and rice wine. Add blanched ribs. Skim foam thoroughly.
Add green papaya cubes. Reduce to a low simmer. Cover partially. Simmer 60–70 minutes until papaya is completely tender, translucent, and silky.
Add wolfberries in the last 10 minutes. Remove ginger. Season with white pepper only. Drizzle sesame oil. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 437.6mg, Na 71.3mg, Phos 149.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Garlic Baked Cod
recipes.description: Herb-crusted cod baked with garlic and lemon
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking tray with parchment paper.
Place carrot rounds and zucchini pieces on the tray. Drizzle 6 ml olive oil over them.
Pat cod fillets dry. Place on top of the vegetables. Drizzle remaining olive oil over the fish.
Scatter minced garlic and fresh parsley over. Squeeze lemon juice evenly.
Bake 18–20 minutes until cod is opaque throughout and flakes cleanly with a fork. Vegetables should be tender with lightly caramelised edges.
Serve immediately directly from the tray.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 711.0mg, Na 86.0mg, Phos 245.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Roasted Chicken with Herbs
recipes.description: Seasoned roasted chicken breast with fresh herbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Pat chicken breasts completely dry — this is essential for a golden exterior.
Whisk olive oil, minced garlic, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, and lemon juice together. Rub all over the chicken breasts.
Place chicken on a roasting rack over a lined tray. Arrange carrot and zucchini pieces around the chicken on the tray.
Roast 22–25 minutes until chicken is golden and internal temperature reaches 74°C. Vegetables should be caramelised at the edges.
Rest 5 minutes before slicing. Serve with the pan-roasted vegetables alongside.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 541.5mg, Na 103.6mg, Phos 260.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Green Bean Casserole
recipes.description: Fresh green beans with light cream sauce
instructions: Heat olive oil in an oven-safe pan over medium heat. Add diced shallot and garlic. Sauté 3 minutes until softened.
Add sliced button mushrooms and fresh thyme. Cook 5 minutes until mushrooms are golden and all moisture has evaporated.
Whisk cornstarch into rice milk until smooth. Pour over mushrooms. Stir continuously over medium heat 3–4 minutes until sauce thickens to a creamy consistency.
Add trimmed green beans. Toss to coat in the sauce.
Transfer to oven at 180°C. Bake uncovered 20–25 minutes until green beans are tender and the top is lightly golden.
Serve as a warm casserole side.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 297.3mg, Na 27.2mg, Phos 74.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Turkey Meatballs
recipes.description: Lean turkey meatballs with herbs, baked until golden
instructions: Combine ground turkey with egg white, unsalted breadcrumbs, minced garlic, diced onion, fresh parsley, and lemon juice in a bowl.
Mix thoroughly but gently — do not overwork or the meatballs will be dense.
With wet hands, roll into 20–24 equal-sized balls.
Heat olive oil in a wide oven-safe pan over medium-high heat. Brown meatballs on all sides, about 3–4 minutes total. Work in batches — do not crowd.
Transfer pan to oven at 190°C. Bake 12–15 minutes until meatballs are cooked through.
Serve immediately with steamed vegetables or white rice.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 306.6mg, Na 100.0mg, Phos 177.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Sweet Potato
recipes.description: Naturally sweet baked potato with herbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Scrub sweet potatoes clean. Pierce the skin 6–8 times with a fork — this allows steam to escape during baking.
Rub the outside of each sweet potato lightly with olive oil.
Place directly on the oven rack. Bake 40–50 minutes until completely soft when pierced with a skewer and the skin is slightly crispy.
Remove from oven. Split open lengthwise. Sprinkle cinnamon and fresh chives over the opening. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 414.0mg, Na 66.2mg, Phos 58.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg White Scramble
recipes.description: Fluffy egg white scramble with fresh vegetables
instructions: Whisk egg whites with a small pinch of black pepper until just combined — do not whip to stiff peaks.
Heat olive oil in a small non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour in egg whites. Cook without stirring 1 minute until edges are set.
Using a spatula, gently fold the egg whites inward from the edges in slow strokes, creating large, soft, glossy curds.
Remove from heat when the egg whites are just set but still slightly shiny — residual heat finishes cooking them.
Scatter chopped fresh chives and parsley over. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 238.2mg, Na 202.2mg, Phos 23.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Rice Pilaf with Vegetables
recipes.description: Fluffy rice with mixed vegetables in light broth
instructions: Heat olive oil in a medium pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and minced garlic. Sauté 3 minutes.
Add white rice. Stir for 1–2 minutes to coat grains in oil and toast lightly.
Add diced carrot and zucchini. Add 340 ml water. Bring to a full boil.
Cover tightly. Reduce to the very lowest heat. Cook 18 minutes without lifting the lid.
Remove from heat. Rest covered 8 minutes.
Uncover. Fluff with a fork. Add fresh parsley and squeeze lemon juice over. Toss gently. Serve.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 263.2mg, Na 22.2mg, Phos 103.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Glazed Pork Tenderloin
recipes.description: Tender pork loin with light honey glaze
instructions: Whisk honey, low-sodium Dijon mustard, minced garlic, fresh thyme, lemon juice, and olive oil together for the glaze.
Trim silver skin from pork tenderloin. Season with black pepper.
Heat a heavy oven-safe pan over high heat. Sear pork 2 minutes per side on all faces until evenly golden.
Brush the glaze generously over the seared pork.
Transfer to oven at 200°C. Roast 15–18 minutes, brushing with remaining glaze at 8 minutes, until internal temperature reaches 63°C.
Rest 6 minutes tented with foil. Slice across the grain and serve with any resting juices.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 470.7mg, Na 75.3mg, Phos 257.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Salmon
recipes.description: Fresh salmon with herbs and lemon
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C. Line a baking dish with parchment paper.
Pat salmon fillets completely dry. Place skin-side down in the dish.
Drizzle olive oil evenly over the flesh. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over each fillet. Scatter minced garlic and fresh dill.
Bake 14–16 minutes depending on thickness — salmon is done when it is opaque throughout and the thickest part flakes gently when pressed with a fork. Do not overbake.
Rest 3 minutes. Serve immediately with steamed asparagus or a green salad.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 395.8mg, Na 60.9mg, Phos 244.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Roasted Turkey Breast
recipes.description: Herb-roasted turkey breast with vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 175°C.
Pat turkey breast completely dry. Mix olive oil, minced garlic, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, and lemon juice. Rub all over the turkey.
Place on a roasting rack skin-side up in a roasting tray.
Roast 35–40 minutes until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part reads 74°C.
Rest 10 minutes before slicing — this is critical for retaining juices.
Slice across the grain into medallions. Drizzle any resting juices over. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 319.5mg, Na 63.8mg, Phos 215.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Asparagus
recipes.description: Fresh asparagus steamed with garlic
instructions: Snap the woody bottom 3–4 cm from each asparagus spear where it naturally breaks.
Bring a pot of water to a rolling boil. Add asparagus and blanch 2–3 minutes depending on thickness — they should be bright green and tender-crisp, not limp.
Drain immediately. Transfer to a serving plate.
Drizzle olive oil and fresh lemon juice evenly over while still hot.
Scatter fresh parsley. Season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 223.6mg, Na 3.6mg, Phos 54.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken Breast with Apple
recipes.description: Tender chicken with apple and sage
instructions: Peel and core apple. Slice into 1 cm wedges. Dice shallot finely.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add chicken breast. Sear 4–5 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove to a plate.
In the same pan, add shallot. Cook 2 minutes until softened. Add apple wedges and fresh thyme.
Add apple cider vinegar and honey. Stir and cook 4–5 minutes until apple is soft and the sauce has reduced to a sticky glaze.
Return chicken. Spoon sauce and apple over the chicken. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 387.6mg, Na 77.2mg, Phos 238.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Vegetable Soup
recipes.description: Hearty vegetable broth with mixed vegetables
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and minced garlic. Sauté 4 minutes until soft.
Add carrot rounds, diced zucchini, green bean pieces, and sliced celery. Stir 2 minutes.
Pour in 1000 ml water. Bring to a boil. Skim foam if any appears.
Reduce to a medium-low simmer. Cook uncovered 20–25 minutes until all vegetables are completely tender.
Add fresh parsley and lemon juice. Season with black pepper.
Ladle into bowls. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 276.4mg, Na 28.4mg, Phos 40.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked White Fish
recipes.description: White fish baked with fresh herbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking dish with parchment.
Pat white fish fillets completely dry. Place in the dish.
Drizzle olive oil evenly over fish. Add minced garlic. Squeeze lemon juice over.
Scatter fresh chopped parsley generously over the fillets.
Bake 14–16 minutes until fish is opaque throughout and flakes cleanly. Do not overbake — white fish continues cooking off the heat.
Serve immediately with steamed vegetables.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 414.0mg, Na 72.6mg, Phos 200.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Roasted Root Vegetables
recipes.description: Roasted carrots and vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 210°C. Line a large baking tray.
Peel parsnip, carrot, and turnip. Peel and quarter beetroot. Cut all into 3 cm chunks.
In a large bowl, toss all vegetables with olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, fresh rosemary, and fresh thyme until every piece is thoroughly coated.
Spread in a single layer on the baking tray — do not crowd or they will steam instead of roast.
Roast 30–35 minutes, turning once at 15 minutes, until deeply golden and caramelised at the edges.
Serve immediately as a warm side dish.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 377.0mg, Na 65.4mg, Phos 54.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Turkey Cutlets
recipes.description: Thin turkey cutlets pan-seared with butter
instructions: Mix olive oil, minced garlic, fresh parsley, and fresh basil together.
Pound turkey breast to an even 1.5 cm thickness using a meat mallet — this ensures even cooking.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Add 8 ml of the herb oil. Add turkey. Cook 3–4 minutes per side until golden and internal temperature reaches 74°C.
Remove turkey. Add lemon juice and remaining herb oil to the pan. Stir, scraping any browned bits from the base.
Drizzle the herb sauce over the turkey. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 370.1mg, Na 68.0mg, Phos 221.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken Noodle Soup
recipes.description: Light broth with chicken and egg noodles
instructions: Heat oil and sauté garlic and onion.
Add chicken and cook lightly.
Add water and bring to boil.
Add carrots, celery, and pasta.
Simmer until pasta is tender.
Finish with parsley.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 237.8mg, Na 52.1mg, Phos 114.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Cod with Dill
recipes.description: Cod fillet with fresh dill and lemon
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking dish with parchment.
Pat cod fillets dry. Place in the dish.
Drizzle olive oil over the fish. Scatter minced garlic evenly. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over.
Scatter fresh dill generously over the fillets.
Bake 14–16 minutes until cod is opaque throughout and flakes cleanly with a fork.
Serve immediately — cod continues cooking after it leaves the oven.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 462.9mg, Na 57.1mg, Phos 210.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Cauliflower
recipes.description: Fresh cauliflower florets steamed
instructions: Break cauliflower into even-sized florets.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Steam cauliflower over boiling water 6–8 minutes until completely tender when pierced with a knife.
Transfer to a serving plate.
Mince garlic finely. Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, and minced garlic together.
Drizzle the lemon-garlic oil over the hot cauliflower. Scatter fresh chopped parsley.
Season with black pepper. Serve immediately while still warm.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 412.2mg, Na 40.4mg, Phos 61.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Herb Roasted Chicken
recipes.description: Whole chicken roasted with fresh herbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Mix olive oil, minced garlic, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, paprika, and lemon juice.
Pat chicken thighs dry. Coat thoroughly in the herb-spice mixture, getting under any loose skin folds.
Place on a wire rack over a lined baking tray. Roast 35–40 minutes until skin is deep golden and internal temperature reads 74°C.
Rest 5 minutes. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 348.1mg, Na 96.2mg, Phos 233.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Grilled Tilapia
recipes.description: Grilled tilapia with herbs
instructions: Mix lime juice, minced garlic, fresh coriander, and olive oil together for the marinade.
Pat tilapia fillets dry. Coat thoroughly in the marinade. Rest 15 minutes.
Heat a grill pan or outdoor grill to medium-high. Brush with oil to prevent sticking.
Grill tilapia 3–4 minutes per side until it releases naturally from the grill surface and is opaque throughout.
Serve immediately with a wedge of lime and fresh coriander.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 349.8mg, Na 59.0mg, Phos 177.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Turkey Meatloaf
recipes.description: Lean turkey meatloaf
instructions: Combine ground turkey with egg white, unsalted breadcrumbs, diced onion, minced garlic, fresh parsley, honey, low-sodium Dijon, and olive oil.
Mix gently but thoroughly. Do not overwork.
Press evenly into a lightly oiled loaf pan. Smooth the top.
Bake at 190°C for 40–45 minutes until cooked through — internal temperature should reach 74°C.
Rest 8 minutes before slicing. The meatloaf will hold its shape better if rested.
Slice and serve with steamed green vegetables.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 420.3mg, Na 146.6mg, Phos 238.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Broccoli with Lemon and Olive Oil
recipes.description: Simply steamed broccoli finished with fresh lemon juice, garlic, and olive oil — clean, bright, and completely sodium-controlled.
instructions: Break broccoli into medium-sized florets. Mince garlic finely.
Bring a pot of water to a boil. Steam broccoli over boiling water 5–7 minutes until completely tender but still bright green.
Transfer to a serving plate.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, and minced garlic together.
Drizzle the lemon-garlic oil over the hot broccoli. Season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
