recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 333.8mg, Na 33.6mg, Phos 70.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken Breast Sandwich
recipes.description: Skinless chicken on wheat bread
instructions: Toast white bread until golden and crispy on both sides.
Cook chicken breast: heat a small pan, brush with a little oil, cook 4–5 minutes per side until golden and internal temperature reaches 74°C. Rest 3 minutes before slicing thinly.
Mix yoghurt, low-sodium Dijon mustard, and lemon juice together until smooth.
Spread the yoghurt mixture generously over one side of the toasted bread.
Arrange sliced chicken over the yoghurt. Serve open-face immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 335.8mg, Na 380.6mg, Phos 271.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Roasted Carrot and Parsnip with Honey Thyme
recipes.description: Honey-glazed roasted carrots and parsnip with fresh thyme — naturally sweet, no salt needed.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking tray with parchment.
Peel carrot and parsnip. Cut into 4–5 cm batons of similar size for even cooking.
Toss with olive oil, honey, fresh thyme, and lemon juice in a bowl until thoroughly coated.
Spread in a single layer on the prepared tray.
Roast 30–35 minutes, turning once at 15 minutes, until vegetables are golden and caramelised at the edges.
Serve immediately as a warm side dish.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 358.1mg, Na 39.9mg, Phos 54.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pan-Seared Fish Fillet
recipes.description: White fish pan-seared with minimal oil
instructions: Pat white fish fillets dry. Press unsalted breadcrumbs gently onto the flesh side of each fillet.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Place fillets breadcrumb-side down. Cook undisturbed 4 minutes until the crumb is deeply golden.
Flip carefully. Cook skin-side 3–4 more minutes until fish is opaque throughout.
Add minced garlic to the pan. Cook 30 seconds. Squeeze lemon juice over. Scatter fresh parsley and dill.
Serve immediately while the breadcrumb crust is still crispy.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 459.0mg, Na 80.0mg, Phos 217.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Turkey Burgers
recipes.description: Lean turkey patties on whole wheat
instructions: Combine ground turkey with egg white, minced garlic, finely diced onion, fresh parsley, and lemon juice.
Mix well. With wet hands, shape into 4 equal patties about 2 cm thick.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Cook patties 5–6 minutes per side until deeply golden and internal temperature reaches 74°C. Do not press down — this squeezes out moisture.
Serve immediately with a squeeze of lemon and cucumber salad.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 405.9mg, Na 137.1mg, Phos 225.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Cod with Vegetables
recipes.description: Cod baked with side vegetables
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking dish with parchment.
Place sliced zucchini, red bell pepper, and halved cherry tomatoes in the base of the dish. Drizzle with 8 ml olive oil.
Place cod fillets on top of the vegetables. Drizzle remaining olive oil over the fish.
Scatter minced garlic and fresh thyme. Squeeze lemon juice over everything.
Bake 18–22 minutes until cod is opaque and the vegetables are tender. The tomatoes will burst and caramelise — this is correct.
Serve directly from the baking dish.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 680.7mg, Na 56.3mg, Phos 225.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg White Frittata
recipes.description: Veggie-filled egg white frittata
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Heat olive oil in a 20 cm oven-safe non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add diced onion and minced garlic. Cook 2 minutes.
Add diced zucchini and red bell pepper. Cook 3 minutes until slightly softened.
Whisk egg whites with fresh parsley and black pepper until just combined. Pour evenly over the vegetables.
Cook on the stovetop 2 minutes until the edges begin to set.
Transfer to the oven. Bake 10–12 minutes until the centre is fully set and lightly golden on top.
Cool 3 minutes. Slice into wedges and serve.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 370.2mg, Na 133.1mg, Phos 48.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Herb Roasted Turkey
recipes.description: Whole turkey roasted with herbs
instructions: Preheat oven to 175°C.
Pat turkey breast dry. Mix olive oil, minced garlic, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, and lemon juice. Rub all over.
Place on a roasting rack. Roast 35–40 minutes until internal temperature reads 74°C.
Rest 8 minutes before slicing across the grain. Serve with pan juices.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 378.9mg, Na 74.7mg, Phos 252.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Zucchini Noodles
recipes.description: Fresh zucchini spiralized and steamed
instructions: Spiralise zucchini into spaghetti-like strands. Place in a colander with a pinch of salt. Stand 10 minutes to draw moisture. Rinse and pat dry.
Make herb sauce: blend fresh basil, fresh parsley, minced garlic, olive oil, and lemon juice until smooth.
Steam zucchini noodles over boiling water for exactly 2 minutes — just warm, not soft.
Transfer to a bowl immediately. Toss with herb sauce until every strand is coated.
Season with black pepper. Serve immediately — zucchini releases moisture as it sits.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 466.6mg, Na 15.9mg, Phos 69.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken Salad
recipes.description: Light chicken salad with vegetables
instructions: Cook chicken breast: poach in simmering water 18–20 minutes until cooked through. Cool and shred into pieces.
Slice celery thinly. Chop fresh dill.
Mix yoghurt with lemon juice and fresh dill until smooth.
Combine shredded chicken and celery in a bowl. Add the yoghurt dressing and toss to coat.
Refrigerate 15–20 minutes before serving to allow the flavours to develop. Serve chilled.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 301.4mg, Na 83.3mg, Phos 202.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken Breast
recipes.description: Simple grilled chicken
instructions: Mix olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, and paprika together for the marinade.
Pat chicken breasts dry. Coat thoroughly in the marinade. Rest 20 minutes.
Preheat a grill pan or outdoor grill to medium-high. Brush with a little oil.
Grill chicken 5–6 minutes per side until golden grill marks form and internal temperature reads 74°C.
Rest 5 minutes before slicing across the grain. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 279.2mg, Na 74.7mg, Phos 223.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Apple
recipes.description: Baked apple with cinnamon
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Core the apples from the top, leaving the base intact, to create a well for the filling.
Mix honey, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and lemon juice. Spoon evenly into each apple cavity.
Place apples upright in a small baking dish. Add 2 tablespoons of water to the base of the dish to create steam.
Bake 35–40 minutes until the apples are completely tender when pierced and the filling is bubbling.
Cool 5 minutes before serving. Serve warm.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 118.2mg, Na 1.5mg, Phos 12.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Open-Face Cottage Cheese and Herb Toast
recipes.description: Toasted white bread topped with low-sodium cottage cheese and fresh herbs — a quick, high-protein, low-phosphorus snack.
instructions: Toast white bread slices until golden and crisp.
While bread is warm, spread a generous layer of low-sodium cottage cheese over each slice.
Finely chop fresh chives and fresh dill. Scatter evenly over the cottage cheese.
Squeeze a few drops of lemon juice over each slice.
Season with black pepper. Serve immediately while bread is still warm and crisp.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 96.4mg, Na 128.7mg, Phos 80.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pasta Primavera
recipes.description: Pasta with fresh vegetables
instructions: Cook white pasta in a large pot of unsalted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 60 ml pasta water before draining. Drain.
Toss diced zucchini, halved cherry tomatoes, and asparagus pieces with 8 ml olive oil. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until golden.
Heat remaining olive oil in a large pan over low heat. Add minced garlic. Cook gently 1–2 minutes until pale gold.
Add drained pasta and reserved pasta water. Toss 1 minute over medium heat.
Add roasted vegetables, fresh basil, and fresh parsley. Add lemon juice. Toss everything together.
Season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 529.7mg, Na 15.1mg, Phos 212.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Turkey Chili
recipes.description: Lean turkey chili with beans
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Add diced onion and garlic, sauté 3 minutes until softened.
Add ground turkey, breaking into small pieces, cook 6–8 minutes until browned through.
Add diced red bell pepper and zucchini, stir-fry 3 minutes.
Season with paprika and cumin, toss to coat everything evenly.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve over white rice.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 426.6mg, Na 87.2mg, Phos 190.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Pear
recipes.description: Baked pear with honey
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Halve the Asian pear and remove the core with a small spoon.
Place cut-side up in a baking dish.
Mix honey, cinnamon, and vanilla extract, spoon over the pear halves.
Add a small squeeze of lemon juice over each half.
Bake 25–30 minutes until tender and lightly golden.
Serve warm as a dessert.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 132.7mg, Na 0.5mg, Phos 12.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: White Fish Tacos
recipes.description: Soft tortillas with baked white fish
instructions: Season white fish fillets with cumin and paprika.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat.
Cook fish 3–4 minutes per side until opaque and lightly browned.
Mix yoghurt with lime juice and fresh coriander to make the sauce.
Shred a small amount of cabbage finely.
Place fish on a plate, top with shredded cabbage, and spoon yoghurt sauce over.
Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 507.6mg, Na 88.4mg, Phos 232.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Spinach
recipes.description: Fresh spinach steamed with garlic
instructions: Bring a pot of water to boil.
Add spinach and blanch for 30 seconds only — it wilts very quickly.
Drain immediately and press out excess water.
Heat olive oil in a small pan, add sliced garlic and cook 30 seconds until golden.
Drizzle garlic oil and lemon juice over the spinach.
Toss gently and serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 579.2mg, Na 79.8mg, Phos 55.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Cauliflower Soup with Herb Oil
recipes.description: Silky blended cauliflower soup — no cream, no stock, just vegetables and herbs.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and garlic, sauté 4 minutes until soft.
Add cauliflower florets and stir to coat in oil.
Pour in water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer covered 20 minutes until cauliflower is very tender.
Blend until completely smooth using a stick blender.
Add lemon juice and season to taste.
Serve topped with fresh chives and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 409.8mg, Na 36.7mg, Phos 65.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Paprika Roasted Chicken Drumsticks
recipes.description: Paprika-rubbed drumsticks; skin removed before eating to control phosphorus.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C.
Mix olive oil, paprika, garlic, thyme, and lemon juice into a paste.
Pat chicken drumsticks dry and coat thoroughly in the spice paste.
Place on a wire rack over a lined baking tray.
Roast 35–40 minutes, turning once at 20 minutes, until golden and cooked through.
Rest 5 minutes. Remove and discard skin before eating.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 338.0mg, Na 114.0mg, Phos 232.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Six-Vegetable Herb Roast
recipes.description: Simple roasted vegetable medley — six consistently low-potassium vegetables.
instructions: Preheat oven to 210°C.
Combine zucchini, red bell pepper, asparagus, green beans, and cherry tomatoes in a large bowl.
Toss with olive oil, lemon juice, and fresh thyme until every piece is coated.
Spread in a single layer on a large baking tray — do not overlap.
Roast 25–30 minutes, turning once at 12 minutes, until edges are golden.
Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 328.5mg, Na 7.6mg, Phos 53.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Bell Pepper Egg White Omelette
recipes.description: Fluffy egg white omelette — bell peppers provide colour, sweetness, and vitamin C.
instructions: Dice red and yellow bell peppers into small pieces.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add bell peppers and sauté 2 minutes until slightly softened.
Whisk egg whites with black pepper and fresh chives.
Pour egg whites evenly over the peppers.
Cook undisturbed on low heat 1.5 minutes until edges set.
Fold in half and slide onto a warm plate. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 373.1mg, Na 152.7mg, Phos 41.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pork Tenderloin with Apple Sauce
recipes.description: Lean pork with homemade zero-sodium apple sauce — classic pairing made renal-safe.
instructions: Trim pork tenderloin and season with black pepper and fresh thyme.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe pan over high heat. Sear pork 2–3 minutes per side until golden all over.
Transfer to oven at 200°C and roast 15–18 minutes until internal temperature reaches 63°C.
Rest 5 minutes, tented with foil.
Meanwhile, combine diced apple, cinnamon, lemon juice, and honey in a small saucepan.
Cook over medium heat 10 minutes, stirring, until apple is completely soft. Mash lightly.
Slice pork and serve with warm apple sauce alongside.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 476.8mg, Na 59.2mg, Phos 224.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: White Rice with Stir-Fried Cabbage
recipes.description: Steamed white rice alongside garlicky stir-fried green cabbage — the most reliable renal meal.
instructions: Cook white rice in unsalted water according to packet instructions. Keep warm.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat.
Add sliced garlic and stir-fry 20 seconds until pale gold.
Add shredded cabbage all at once. Toss vigorously over high heat 4 minutes until wilted with slight char.
Add rice vinegar and toss 30 seconds.
Season with black pepper. Serve cabbage alongside the white rice.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 238.7mg, Na 20.4mg, Phos 96.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Lemon Garlic Prawn Skewers
recipes.description: Grilled prawn skewers — portioned for phosphorus management, marinated in lemon and garlic.
instructions: Peel and devein prawns. Thread onto pre-soaked wooden skewers.
Whisk together lemon juice, minced garlic, olive oil, paprika, and black pepper.
Brush marinade generously over all surfaces of the prawns. Marinate 10 minutes.
Heat a grill pan or BBQ to medium-high. Brush lightly with oil.
Grill skewers 2–3 minutes per side until prawns are pink and have slight char marks.
Scatter fresh parsley and serve immediately with a lemon wedge.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 254.2mg, Na 86.7mg, Phos 188.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Blueberry Oat Breakfast Muffins
recipes.description: Low-sodium muffins with blueberries and oats — egg whites and rice milk reduce phosphorus.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C. Line a 12-hole muffin tray with paper cases.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, rolled oats, low-sodium baking powder, and cinnamon.
In a separate bowl, whisk egg whites, honey, canola oil, vanilla extract, and rice milk until smooth.
Pour wet mixture into dry and fold gently — do not overmix, a few lumps are fine.
Fold in blueberries with 3–4 gentle strokes.
Divide batter evenly into the 12 cases.
Bake 18–20 minutes until a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean.
Cool in tray 5 minutes then transfer to a wire rack.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 54.4mg, Na 12.4mg, Phos 42.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Cucumber and Radish Salad
recipes.description: Cold cucumber and radish salad with lemon dressing — both vegetables are very low in minerals.
instructions: Slice cucumber into thin rounds and red radish into thin slices.
Whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, and honey until honey is fully dissolved.
Arrange cucumber and radish slices on a serving plate.
Drizzle dressing evenly over the vegetables.
Scatter fresh mint leaves on top.
Refrigerate 10 minutes before serving to allow flavours to develop.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 226.8mg, Na 18.2mg, Phos 28.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Herb Pasta with Olive Oil
recipes.description: Aglio e olio style — garlic, fresh herbs, olive oil, no salt or cheese.
instructions: Cook spaghetti in a large pot of unsalted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 60 ml pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, heat olive oil in a large pan over low heat.
Add sliced garlic and fry gently 2–3 minutes until pale gold — do not burn.
Add drained pasta and reserved pasta water. Toss vigorously 1 minute to emulsify.
Add fresh parsley and basil. Toss to combine.
Add lemon juice and season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 266.6mg, Na 10.5mg, Phos 168.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pan-Seared Salmon with Fresh Dill
recipes.description: Pan-seared salmon with dill and lemon — controlled portion, omega-3 rich.
instructions: Pat salmon dry and season lightly.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Place salmon skin-side down and sear until crispy.
Flip and cook until just done.
Add lemon juice and sprinkle fresh dill before serving.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 366.6mg, Na 55.7mg, Phos 219.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Halibut with Lemon and Parsley
recipes.description: Simple oven-baked halibut — clean flavour, minimal ingredients, maximum renal safety.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking dish with parchment.
Pat halibut fillets completely dry. Place in the prepared dish.
Drizzle olive oil evenly over each fillet. Squeeze fresh lemon juice over.
Scatter fresh chopped parsley generously over the top.
Bake 15–18 minutes depending on thickness — halibut is done when completely opaque and the flesh separates into clean flakes when gently pressed.
Rest 2 minutes. Serve immediately — halibut dries out quickly if overcooked.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 467.5mg, Na 70.4mg, Phos 269.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Turkey and Vegetable Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Quick turkey stir-fry with low-potassium vegetables — Western protein, simple cooking.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a pan.
Add garlic and onion, sauté until fragrant.
Add ground turkey and cook until browned.
Add zucchini and bell pepper.
Stir-fry until vegetables are tender.
Add lemon juice and thyme, toss and serve.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 621.1mg, Na 116.8mg, Phos 260.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Poached Salmon with Cucumber Sauce
recipes.description: Gently poached salmon with a zero-sodium cucumber-dill sauce.
instructions: Bring water to a gentle simmer.
Add salmon and poach until cooked.
Blend cucumber, yoghurt, lemon juice, and dill.
Serve salmon with chilled cucumber sauce.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 469.5mg, Na 70.6mg, Phos 260.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Roasted Cauliflower Steaks
recipes.description: Thick-cut cauliflower steaks roasted until golden — a low-mineral plant-based main.
instructions: Preheat oven to 220°C. Line a large baking tray with parchment.
Remove outer leaves from cauliflower. Trim the base. Slice two 2.5 cm thick steaks from the centre of the head.
Whisk olive oil, minced garlic, paprika, lemon juice, and black pepper. Brush both sides of each steak generously.
Place on the tray. Roast 15 minutes. Flip carefully using a wide spatula. Roast 10–12 more minutes until both sides are deeply golden.
Scatter fresh parsley. Serve immediately — the caramelised crust is the defining feature.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 503.7mg, Na 48.3mg, Phos 73.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken and Rice One-Pot
recipes.description: Simple one-pot chicken and rice — no added salt, herbs provide all the seasoning.
instructions: Heat oil, sauté garlic and onion.
Add chicken pieces and lightly brown.
Add rice and stir.
Add carrot and water.
Cover and simmer until rice is cooked.
Finish with thyme.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 349.7mg, Na 67.6mg, Phos 233.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Herb-Marinated Grilled Fish
recipes.description: Simple marinated and grilled white fish — works with any firm white fish fillet.
instructions: Mix olive oil, minced garlic, fresh parsley, and lemon juice together for the marinade.
Pat white fish fillets dry. Coat thoroughly on both sides. Marinate 15 minutes.
Heat a grill pan or outdoor grill to medium-high. Brush lightly with oil.
Grill fish 3–4 minutes per side until opaque throughout and grill marks form. Fish releases naturally from the grill surface when it is ready.
Serve immediately with a wedge of lemon and a green salad.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 414.0mg, Na 72.6mg, Phos 200.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Zucchini Noodles with Herb Pesto
recipes.description: Spiralised zucchini noodles with nut-free herb pesto — almost zero phosphorus pasta.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Chop mixed vegetables evenly.
Toss with olive oil and minced garlic.
Roast until tender and slightly caramelized.
Finish with lemon juice before serving.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 466.6mg, Na 15.9mg, Phos 69.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Egg White and Herb Frittata
recipes.description: Oven-baked frittata with five herbs and vegetables — zero yolk phosphorus.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Heat olive oil in a 20 cm oven-safe non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add diced onion and minced garlic. Cook 2 minutes.
Add diced zucchini and red bell pepper. Cook 3 minutes until slightly softened.
Whisk egg whites with fresh parsley and black pepper. Pour evenly over vegetables.
Cook on the stovetop 2 minutes until the edges begin to set.
Transfer to the oven. Bake 10–12 minutes until the centre is fully set and lightly golden.
Cool 3 minutes. Slice into wedges. Serve warm.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 370.2mg, Na 133.1mg, Phos 48.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Chicken with Lemon and Garlic
recipes.description: The most versatile baked chicken recipe — simple, lean, and completely sodium-free.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, and fresh thyme together for the marinade.
Pat chicken breasts completely dry. Coat in the marinade. Marinate 20 minutes.
Place in a baking dish. Bake 22–25 minutes until internal temperature reaches 74°C.
Rest 5 minutes before slicing. The resting step is essential — it redistributes juices.
Slice across the grain and serve immediately with the pan juices drizzled over.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 295.6mg, Na 75.2mg, Phos 230.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Turkey Meatball and Zucchini Soup
recipes.description: Homemade turkey meatballs in a clear vegetable soup — completely sodium-controlled.
instructions: Form turkey meatballs: mix ground turkey with egg white, unsalted breadcrumbs, minced garlic, and fresh parsley. Shape into walnut-sized balls.
Heat olive oil in a large pot. Brown meatballs in batches 2 minutes per side. Remove.
In the same pot, add diced carrot and cook 2 minutes. Add 1000 ml water and bring to a boil.
Return meatballs. Add diced zucchini. Simmer uncovered 20 minutes.
Season with black pepper. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 366.5mg, Na 87.9mg, Phos 138.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Cod with Lemon Herb Sauce
recipes.description: Steamed cod with a bright lemon-herb sauce — no salt at any stage.
instructions: Pat cod fillets dry. Place on a heatproof plate.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly.
Steam 12–15 minutes until cod is completely opaque and flakes gently at the thickest part.
While cod steams, whisk remaining olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, fresh dill, and parsley to make the herb sauce.
Transfer cod to plates. Spoon herb sauce generously over each fillet. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 485.1mg, Na 59.3mg, Phos 212.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Watermelon and Cucumber Salad
recipes.description: Ultra-low mineral summer salad — both ingredients are among the most renal-safe available.
instructions: Cut watermelon into 2.5 cm cubes, removing all seeds. Slice cucumber.
Whisk lime juice and honey until honey dissolves.
Combine watermelon and cucumber in a bowl. Drizzle with the lime-honey dressing. Toss gently.
Scatter fresh mint leaves.
Refrigerate 5 minutes before serving. Serve cold.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 206.9mg, Na 3.0mg, Phos 25.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Roasted Pork with Apple and Thyme
recipes.description: Simple roasted pork loin with apple slices and thyme — no condiments needed.
instructions: Trim pork loin chops of visible fat. Season with black pepper and fresh thyme.
Mix olive oil, minced garlic, and lemon juice. Rub over chops. Marinate 15 minutes.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high. Grill chops 4–5 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 63°C.
While chops rest, make apple chutney: combine diced apple, apple cider vinegar, honey, and a pinch of cinnamon in a small saucepan. Cook 12–15 minutes until apple is completely soft and sticky.
Rest chops 4 minutes. Serve with warm apple chutney alongside.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 427.1mg, Na 76.1mg, Phos 230.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Egg Cups with Herbs
recipes.description: Individual baked egg white cups with fresh herbs — elegant breakfast or snack.
instructions: Preheat oven to 180°C. Grease 4 ramekins with a little olive oil.
Whisk egg whites with chopped fresh chives, fresh dill, a tiny amount of minced garlic, and black pepper.
Pour evenly into the 4 prepared ramekins.
Bake 15–18 minutes until the egg whites are fully set and slightly golden on top.
Rest 2 minutes in the ramekins. Serve in the ramekins immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 130.1mg, Na 101.4mg, Phos 13.7mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pasta with Roasted Vegetables
recipes.description: White pasta tossed with roasted low-potassium vegetables and fresh herbs.
instructions: Toss diced zucchini and red bell pepper with 8 ml olive oil and black pepper. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes until golden.
Cook white pasta in a large pot of unsalted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 60 ml pasta water. Drain.
Heat remaining olive oil in a large pan over low heat. Add minced garlic. Cook gently 1 minute until pale gold.
Add pasta and reserved pasta water. Toss over medium heat 1 minute.
Add roasted vegetables and fresh basil. Add lemon juice. Toss everything together.
Season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 419.2mg, Na 11.2mg, Phos 180.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken Caesar Wrap (Modified)
recipes.description: Modified Caesar wrap using lettuce instead of tortilla — eliminates tortilla sodium.
instructions: Cook chicken breast: heat a small pan, brush with a tiny amount of olive oil. Cook 4–5 minutes per side until golden and internal temperature reaches 74°C. Rest 3 minutes. Slice thinly.
Whisk yoghurt, low-sodium Dijon mustard, minced garlic, and lemon juice together until smooth.
Toss sliced chicken with the yoghurt dressing to coat.
Lay large iceberg lettuce leaves flat as wraps. Fill each with dressed chicken.
Drizzle remaining olive oil over. Wrap and serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 284.0mg, Na 86.7mg, Phos 216.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Grilled Chicken Thighs with Herb Oil
recipes.description: Grilled boneless thighs with herb oil — skin removed before eating.
instructions: Mix olive oil, minced garlic, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, and lemon juice. Coat chicken thighs thoroughly. Marinate 20 minutes.
Preheat grill to medium-high.
Grill thighs 6–8 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 74°C and the skin is golden and slightly charred.
Rest 5 minutes. Remove and discard skin before eating — this is the key step for CKD suitability.
Serve immediately drizzled with any resting herb oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 261.4mg, Na 76.4mg, Phos 184.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked White Fish with Fennel
recipes.description: Oven-baked fish with fennel — fennel has a very low mineral profile and provides natural sweetness.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking dish with parchment.
Slice fennel bulb into 1 cm rounds, removing the tough core.
Arrange fennel slices in the base of the dish. Drizzle with 5 ml olive oil.
Pat white fish fillets dry. Place on top of the fennel.
Drizzle remaining olive oil and lemon juice over the fish. Scatter fresh dill.
Bake 18–22 minutes until fish is opaque throughout and fennel is completely tender.
Serve together — the fennel juices form a natural sauce at the base.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 654.6mg, Na 102.9mg, Phos 228.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Turkey Lettuce Wraps with Mango Salsa
recipes.description: Lean turkey in lettuce wraps with low-potassium mango salsa — colourful and satisfying.
instructions: Cook ground turkey in olive oil over medium-high heat, breaking into small pieces, 6–8 minutes until fully browned. Season with black pepper.
Make mango salsa: combine diced mango, finely sliced shallot, lime juice, fresh coriander, and black pepper. Mix well.
Wash and chill large iceberg lettuce leaves.
Spoon warm turkey into chilled lettuce cups. Top with mango salsa.
Serve immediately — the contrast of warm turkey and cold crisp lettuce is essential.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 402.2mg, Na 104.4mg, Phos 201.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Lemon Herb Roasted Potatoes (Leached)
recipes.description: Crispy roasted potatoes with potassium leached out — portion-controlled renal adaptation.
instructions: LEACHING STEP — do this 2 hours before cooking: peel and dice potato into 2 cm cubes. Cover generously with cold water. Soak 2 hours, changing the water once at 1 hour. This removes approximately 50% of potassium. Drain. Rinse. Pat completely dry.
Preheat oven to 220°C. Place a large baking tray in the oven to preheat — a hot tray produces crispier potatoes.
Toss leached, dried potato cubes with olive oil, minced garlic, fresh rosemary, and lemon juice.
Carefully spread on the preheated hot tray.
Roast 30–35 minutes, turning once at 15 minutes, until deeply golden and crispy. Season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 206.6mg, Na 11.4mg, Phos 42.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Chicken and Asparagus Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Quick pan stir-fry combining two renal-safe ingredients.
instructions: Slice chicken breast thinly across the grain. Season with black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add chicken. Cook 3–4 minutes without moving to develop a sear. Toss and stir-fry 1 more minute until just cooked. Remove to a plate.
Add minced garlic to the same pan. Cook 20 seconds. Add asparagus pieces.
Stir-fry asparagus over high heat 3–4 minutes until tender-crisp with slight char marks.
Return chicken. Add lemon juice and fresh thyme. Toss everything together 30 seconds.
Season with black pepper. Serve over white rice.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 413.3mg, Na 69.1mg, Phos 245.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Herb-Roasted Vegetables with Lemon
recipes.description: Classic roasted vegetable side with fresh herbs and lemon.
instructions: Preheat oven to 210°C. Line a large baking tray.
Combine cauliflower florets, green bean pieces, sliced zucchini, and asparagus pieces in a large bowl.
Toss with olive oil, minced garlic, and lemon juice until every piece is coated.
Spread in a strict single layer on the tray.
Roast 25–30 minutes, turning once at 12 minutes, until golden at the edges.
Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 323.6mg, Na 17.1mg, Phos 55.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Salmon Patties (Low-Sodium)
recipes.description: Pan-fried salmon cakes made without canned salmon — uses fresh poached salmon.
instructions: Poach fresh salmon in simmering water 8 minutes until opaque. Drain. Flake into a bowl, removing any bones.
Add unsalted breadcrumbs, egg white, lemon juice, fresh dill, minced garlic, and black pepper. Mix gently to combine.
Shape into 6 equal patties. Refrigerate 15 minutes to firm up — this helps them hold together.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Cook patties 3–4 minutes per side until golden on both sides.
Serve with a fresh cucumber salad and a wedge of lemon.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 229.9mg, Na 52.3mg, Phos 135.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Cauliflower and Leek Soup
recipes.description: Smooth cauliflower soup with leek — low-mineral, naturally creamy.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add sliced leek white parts and minced garlic. Sauté 4 minutes until soft.
Add cauliflower florets. Stir 2 minutes.
Pour in 700 ml water. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat. Simmer covered 20 minutes until cauliflower is very tender.
Blend until completely smooth. Add lemon juice. Season with black pepper.
Serve topped with fresh chives and a drizzle of olive oil.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 376.4mg, Na 37.1mg, Phos 60.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Baked Tilapia with Mango Salsa
recipes.description: Simple baked tilapia topped with fresh mango salsa — tropical and vibrant.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C. Line a baking dish with parchment.
Pat tilapia fillets dry. Place in the dish. Drizzle with olive oil. Season with black pepper.
Bake 14–16 minutes until tilapia is opaque and flakes easily.
While fish bakes, make mango salsa: combine diced mango, finely sliced shallot, lime juice, and fresh coriander.
Remove fish from oven. Top immediately with the fresh mango salsa. Serve straight from the dish.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 422.2mg, Na 62.4mg, Phos 180.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Herb Chicken Skewers with Yoghurt Dip
recipes.description: Grilled herb chicken skewers with a zero-sodium yoghurt-herb dipping sauce.
instructions: Mix olive oil, lemon juice, paprika, and minced garlic as a marinade. Coat chicken breast cubes. Marinate 20 minutes.
Thread chicken onto pre-soaked wooden skewers.
Grill on medium-high heat 10–12 minutes, turning frequently, until cooked through and slightly charred.
Make yoghurt dip: combine yoghurt, fresh dill, and finely grated and squeezed cucumber. Mix well.
Serve hot skewers with the cold yoghurt dip alongside.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 408.6mg, Na 98.3mg, Phos 278.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Warm Quinoa Salad with Roasted Vegetables
recipes.description: Warm quinoa base with roasted low-potassium vegetables — plant protein dish.
instructions: Cook quinoa in unsalted water according to packet instructions. Drain.
Toss diced zucchini and red bell pepper with 8 ml olive oil. Roast at 200°C for 20 minutes.
Whisk remaining olive oil with lemon juice for the dressing.
Combine warm quinoa with the roasted vegetables. Add dressing and fresh parsley. Toss well.
Season with black pepper. Serve warm.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 379.4mg, Na 13.9mg, Phos 146.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pan-Seared Chicken with Herb Pan Sauce
recipes.description: Seared chicken breast with fresh herb pan sauce made from deglazing with lemon.
instructions: Pat chicken breasts dry. Season with black pepper.
Heat olive oil in a heavy-bottomed skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken 5–6 minutes per side until golden and internal temperature reads 74°C. Remove to a plate to rest.
Add minced garlic to the same hot pan. Cook 20 seconds. Add fresh thyme.
Add lemon juice and water. Scrape up all the browned bits from the base of the pan — these are the flavour.
Simmer 2 minutes until slightly reduced. Stir in fresh parsley.
Slice chicken across the grain. Pour herb pan sauce over immediately. Serve.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 319.2mg, Na 77.8mg, Phos 231.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Vegetable and Egg White Fried Rice
recipes.description: Western-style fried rice with egg whites and low-potassium vegetables.
instructions: Break up cold day-old white rice thoroughly.
Blanch diced green beans in boiling water 2 minutes. Drain.
Heat olive oil in a wok over high heat. Scramble egg whites in slow strokes into large, soft curds. Remove.
Add diced red bell pepper and blanched green beans. Stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add rice. Press against wok surface 1 minute without stirring. Then toss and stir-fry 2 minutes.
Return egg whites. Add spring onion. Toss. Add lemon juice. Toss once more.
Season with black pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 282.7mg, Na 80.9mg, Phos 94.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Poached Egg on Cauliflower Mash
recipes.description: Creamy cauliflower mash topped with poached egg — elegant low-phosphorus brunch.
instructions: Steam cauliflower until very tender. Drain. Mash thoroughly with olive oil, lemon juice, and black pepper until smooth.
Fill a shallow pan with 5 cm of water. Add white vinegar. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Crack egg into a small cup. Create a gentle swirl in the water. Slide egg in.
Poach 3 minutes for a runny yolk. Remove with a slotted spoon. Pat dry with paper towel.
Spoon cauliflower mash onto a plate. Place poached egg on top.
Scatter fresh chives. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 544.4mg, Na 113.6mg, Phos 178.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Grilled Pork Chops with Apple Chutney
recipes.description: Lean grilled pork chops with homemade no-salt apple chutney.
instructions: Make apple chutney: combine diced apple, apple cider vinegar, honey, and a pinch of cinnamon in a small saucepan. Cook over medium heat 12–15 minutes until apple is completely soft and liquid has reduced to a sticky glaze. Set aside.
Brush pork loin chops with olive oil and fresh thyme. Season with black pepper.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high. Grill chops 4–5 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 63°C.
Rest 4 minutes. Serve with warm apple chutney spooned alongside.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 493.5mg, Na 84.1mg, Phos 252.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Lemon Herb Baked Salmon
recipes.description: Oven-baked salmon with a generous herb crust — no salt, omega-3 rich.
instructions: Preheat oven to 190°C. Line a baking dish with parchment.
Mix fresh dill, fresh parsley, lemon juice, minced garlic, and olive oil together for the herb crust.
Pat salmon fillets dry. Place skin-side down in the dish. Spread herb mixture over the flesh side.
Bake 14–16 minutes until internal temperature reaches 60–63°C. The salmon should be opaque with a slightly translucent centre.
Rest 3 minutes. Serve immediately with steamed green beans.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 398.3mg, Na 58.7mg, Phos 223.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Roasted Turkey with Herb Gravy
recipes.description: Herb-roasted turkey breast with a homemade unsalted pan gravy.
instructions: Preheat oven to 175°C. Rub turkey breast with olive oil, minced garlic, fresh rosemary, fresh thyme, and lemon juice.
Place on a roasting rack. Roast 35–40 minutes until internal temperature reads 74°C.
Remove turkey to a plate. Rest 10 minutes.

Make herb gravy: whisk cornstarch into 200 ml cold water until smooth. Pour into the roasting pan over medium heat on the stovetop. Stir continuously, scraping up all browned bits, until the gravy thickens.
Slice turkey across the grain. Serve with herb gravy poured over.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 321.2mg, Na 64.0mg, Phos 215.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Tom Yum Soup
recipes.description: Traditional Thai soup with lemongrass, lime, and herbs
instructions: Bring 700 ml low-sodium broth to a boil in a medium pot. Add bruised lemongrass, sliced galangal, and torn kaffir lime leaves. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add sliced button mushrooms and halved cherry tomatoes. Simmer 4 minutes.
Add prawns. Simmer gently 3–4 minutes until pink and cooked through.
Remove and discard lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Taste — it should be savoury, sour, and aromatic.
Ladle into bowls. Scatter fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 420.5mg, Na 308.0mg, Phos 165.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad Thai Vegetables
recipes.description: Thai stir-fried noodles with fresh vegetables
instructions: Soak rice noodles in cold water 20 minutes. Drain.
Cook noodles in unsalted boiling water 2 minutes. Drain. Rinse under cold water.
Heat rice bran oil in a wok over high heat. Add minced garlic. Stir-fry 20 seconds.
Add sliced chicken breast. Stir-fry 4–5 minutes until cooked through.
Add bean sprouts and spring onion. Toss 1 minute.
Add noodles. Toss 1 minute. Push to one side. Add egg whites and scramble into small curds.
Mix everything together. Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds.
Scatter fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 334.4mg, Na 224.7mg, Phos 216.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Green Curry
recipes.description: Aromatic green curry with chicken and vegetables
instructions: Heat rice bran oil in a wok over medium heat. Add red curry paste. Stir-fry 1 minute until fragrant and the paste darkens slightly.
Add sliced chicken breast. Toss to coat in the paste. Cook 3 minutes.
Add sliced zucchini and green bean pieces. Stir to combine.
Add 250 ml water, bruised lemongrass, and kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a simmer.
Add low-sodium fish sauce. Simmer 10 minutes until chicken is cooked and vegetables are tender.
Stir in lime juice and fresh Thai basil. Remove lemongrass and lime leaves before serving.
Serve over steamed white rice.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 283.8mg, Na 70.6mg, Phos 134.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Basil Chicken
recipes.description: Quickly stir-fried chicken with fragrant Thai basil
instructions: Heat rice bran oil in a wok over high heat. Add minced garlic. Stir-fry 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add diced chicken breast. Stir-fry 5–6 minutes until fully cooked through.
Add diced red bell pepper. Stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds.
Remove from heat immediately. Fold in fresh Thai basil leaves — the residual heat wilts them in 20 seconds.
Serve over steamed white rice immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 258.7mg, Na 64.4mg, Phos 163.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Steamed Shrimp with Herbs
recipes.description: Tender shrimp steamed with aromatic herbs
instructions: Bruise lemongrass stalks with the flat of a knife. Slice into rounds.
Arrange lemongrass, sliced galangal, and kaffir lime leaves on a heatproof plate.
Place prawns on top of the aromatics.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly.
Steam over high heat 6–8 minutes until prawns are pink throughout.
Discard all aromatics. Drizzle lime juice over. Scatter fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 242.5mg, Na 76.3mg, Phos 166.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Coconut Rice
recipes.description: Fragrant rice cooked in lite coconut milk
instructions: Wash rice until water runs clear. Drain.
Bring light coconut milk and water to a gentle simmer with bruised lemongrass and kaffir lime leaf. Steep 5 minutes off the heat.
Strain. Measure the infused liquid — top up with water to 280 ml if needed.
Combine rice and infused liquid. Bring to a boil over high heat. Stir once.
Cover tightly. Reduce to lowest heat. Cook 15 minutes.
Off heat, rest covered 8 minutes. Fluff with a fork. Serve.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 217.7mg, Na 10.4mg, Phos 117.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Cucumber Salad
recipes.description: Refreshing cucumber salad with peanut sauce
instructions: Slice cucumber into thin rounds. Thinly slice shallot.
Whisk lime juice, honey, and rice bran oil together until honey dissolves.
Combine cucumber and shallot in a bowl. Pour dressing over. Toss.
Scatter fresh coriander.
Refrigerate 10 minutes before serving. Serve cold.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 214.2mg, Na 5.3mg, Phos 32.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Ginger Fish Cakes
recipes.description: Thai fish cakes flavored with ginger
instructions: Process white fish in a food processor to a coarse paste. Transfer to a bowl.
Add finely minced lemongrass, shredded kaffir lime leaf, minced garlic, egg white, cornstarch, low-sodium fish sauce, and chopped coriander. Stir vigorously in one direction 2 minutes until cohesive.
With wet hands, shape into 12 small patties.
Heat rice bran oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Cook patties 3 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Serve with sliced cucumber and lime wedge.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 239.6mg, Na 59.0mg, Phos 106.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Red Curry
recipes.description: Classic Thai red curry with vegetables
instructions: Heat rice bran oil in a wok over medium heat. Add red curry paste. Stir-fry 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sliced chicken breast. Toss to coat. Cook 3 minutes.
Add sliced zucchini and red bell pepper.
Add 200 ml water and kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a simmer.
Add low-sodium fish sauce. Simmer 8–10 minutes until chicken is cooked through.
Stir in lime juice and fresh Thai basil. Remove kaffir lime leaves.
Serve over steamed white rice.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 503.7mg, Na 132.7mg, Phos 237.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad See Ew
recipes.description: Thai dark soy stir-fried noodles
instructions: Separate fresh rice noodles gently.
Whisk low-sodium soy sauce, low-sodium fish sauce, and honey for the sauce.
Scramble egg white in a hot wok with a little rice bran oil. Remove.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry minced garlic 15 seconds. Add sliced chicken. Cook 3 minutes.
Add blanched gai lan. Stir-fry 1 minute.
Add noodles. Press against wok 45 seconds. Toss. Pour sauce over. Toss to coat.
Return egg white. Toss 20 seconds. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 340.4mg, Na 263.4mg, Phos 195.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Garlic Shrimp
recipes.description: Quick-fried shrimp with garlic and chilies
instructions: Pat prawns completely dry.
Heat rice bran oil in a wok over high heat until smoking. Add minced garlic. Stir-fry 30–40 seconds until golden.
Add prawns in a single layer. Cook undisturbed 60 seconds. Flip.
Cook 45 more seconds until pink and just cooked through.
Add low-sodium fish sauce. Toss 15 seconds. Remove from heat.
Add lime juice and fresh coriander leaves. Serve over white rice.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 256.2mg, Na 106.7mg, Phos 192.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Lemongrass Chicken Soup
recipes.description: Fragrant soup with lemongrass and chicken
instructions: Bring 800 ml water to a boil with lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add sliced button mushrooms. Simmer 4 minutes.
Add chicken breast pieces. Reduce to a gentle simmer. Cook 15 minutes until fully cooked.
Remove and discard lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice.
Scatter fresh coriander. Serve hot immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 401.0mg, Na 80.6mg, Phos 206.1mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad Krapow Moo
recipes.description: Thai basil pork stir-fry
instructions: Heat oil in wok over high heat (1 min).
Add garlic, stir 10–15 sec until fragrant.
Add chicken, stir-fry 3–4 min until cooked.
Add vegetables, cook 2–3 min.
Add sauce and toss 1 min.
Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 335.5mg, Na 68.5mg, Phos 163.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Eggplant Stir-Fry
recipes.description: Purple eggplant with Thai basil
instructions: Slice eggplant into 2 cm rounds. Place in a colander with a pinch of salt. Stand 15 minutes. Rinse thoroughly. Pat dry.
Whisk rice wine, rice vinegar, honey, and lime juice together as a glaze.
Heat 15 ml canola oil in a wide pan over medium-high. Add eggplant in a single layer. Fry 3 minutes per side until golden and softened. Remove.
Add remaining 5 ml oil. Stir-fry minced garlic and grated ginger 20 seconds.
Return eggplant. Add glaze. Toss and cook 1–2 minutes until sauce reduces to coat each piece.
Remove from heat. Add low-sodium fish sauce. Fold in fresh Thai basil. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 200.9mg, Na 12.6mg, Phos 26.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Coconut Fish Soup
recipes.description: Creamy coconut soup with fresh fish
instructions: Bring 400 ml water to a simmer with lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves. Simmer 8 minutes.
Add light coconut milk. Stir to combine and bring back to a gentle simmer.
Add white fish cubes. Simmer gently 6–8 minutes until fish is opaque throughout.
Remove and discard lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Taste and adjust sourness.
Scatter fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 528.4mg, Na 87.4mg, Phos 209.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Vegetable Spring Rolls
recipes.description: Fresh spring rolls with vegetables
instructions: Cook rice noodles in unsalted water until just tender. Drain. Rinse under cold water. Cool completely.
Blanch bean sprouts 30 seconds in boiling water. Drain and cool.
Whisk lime juice, honey, and rice bran oil together for the dressing.
Combine cooled noodles, bean sprouts, carrot julienne, and cucumber.
Pour dressing over and toss well.
Fold in fresh mint and fresh coriander. Serve cold immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 214.0mg, Na 81.9mg, Phos 57.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Gaeng Massaman
recipes.description: Rich Massaman curry with chicken
instructions: Heat rice bran oil in a pot over medium heat. Add diced onion and minced garlic. Cook 3 minutes.
Add curry paste. Stir-fry 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sliced chicken breast. Toss to coat. Cook 3 minutes.
Add leached diced potato. Stir to combine.
Add 300 ml water and light coconut milk. Bring to a simmer.
Add low-sodium fish sauce. Simmer 20–25 minutes until potato is completely tender and chicken is cooked.
Stir in lime juice. Serve over white rice.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 325.1mg, Na 88.2mg, Phos 172.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Crab Cakes
recipes.description: Thai-style crab cakes with lime
instructions: Combine crab meat with finely minced lemongrass, shredded kaffir lime leaf, minced garlic, egg white, cornstarch, low-sodium fish sauce, and chopped coriander.
Mix well. Shape into 9 equal patties with wet hands.
Heat rice bran oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Cook crab cakes 3 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Serve with sliced cucumber and lime wedge.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 230.6mg, Na 229.5mg, Phos 133.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad Woon Sen
recipes.description: Thai glass noodle stir-fry
instructions: Soak glass noodles in hot water 15 minutes. Drain. Cut into 15 cm lengths.
Heat rice bran oil in a wok over high heat. Add minced garlic. Stir-fry 20 seconds.
Add sliced chicken. Stir-fry 3 minutes until cooked.
Add sliced carrot and celery. Stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add glass noodles. Toss vigorously 2 minutes.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds.
Scatter fresh coriander and spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 322.6mg, Na 186.3mg, Phos 144.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Pineapple Fried Rice
recipes.description: Fried rice served in pineapple shell
instructions: Break up cold day-old cooked white rice. Dice pineapple.
Scramble egg whites in 5 ml rice bran oil in a hot wok. Remove.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry minced garlic 20 seconds.
Add rice. Press against wok surface 2 minutes without stirring. Toss.
Add diced pineapple. Toss 1 minute.
Return egg whites. Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds.
Scatter spring onion and coriander. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 139.3mg, Na 47.2mg, Phos 56.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad Pak
recipes.description: Stir-fried mixed vegetables Thai style
instructions: Blanch bok choy in boiling water 1.5 minutes. Drain. Trim snow peas.
Heat wok over high heat. Add rice bran oil.
Add sliced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry 20 seconds.
Add snow peas. Stir-fry 1.5 minutes. Add bean sprouts and blanched bok choy. Stir-fry 1.5 minutes.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 305.2mg, Na 52.8mg, Phos 72.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Fish Soup
recipes.description: Spicy fish soup with lime and herbs
instructions: Bring 700 ml water to a boil with lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add sliced button mushrooms. Simmer 4 minutes.
Add white fish cubes. Reduce to a gentle simmer. Cook 6–8 minutes until fish is opaque.
Remove and discard all aromatics.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice.
Scatter fresh coriander. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 521.4mg, Na 87.6mg, Phos 211.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Satay Chicken
recipes.description: Grilled chicken with peanut sauce
instructions: Blend lemongrass, garlic, galangal, honey, low-sodium fish sauce, lime juice, and rice bran oil into a smooth marinade.
Coat chicken breast pieces in marinade. Marinate 20 minutes.
Thread onto pre-soaked skewers. Grill on medium-high 10–12 minutes, turning frequently, until cooked through.
While chicken grills, slice cucumber thinly for the relish.
Serve skewers with sliced cucumber and extra lime.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 405.0mg, Na 96.7mg, Phos 246.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Papaya Salad
recipes.description: Spicy green papaya salad
instructions: Shred green papaya with a julienne peeler or box grater.
Blanch green beans 1.5 minutes. Drain and cool.
Pound garlic and chilli together lightly.
Whisk lime juice, honey, and low-sodium fish sauce until honey dissolves.
Combine papaya, halved cherry tomatoes, green beans in a bowl. Pour dressing over. Toss.
Scatter unsalted peanuts and fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 230.4mg, Na 18.9mg, Phos 28.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad Pak Bung
recipes.description: Stir-fried morning glory Thai style
instructions: Blanch water spinach in boiling water exactly 1 minute. Drain thoroughly.
Heat wok over the highest heat until smoking. Add rice bran oil.
Add minced garlic and sliced chilli. Stir-fry 20 seconds until garlic turns golden.
Add blanched water spinach. Toss vigorously over maximum heat 1.5–2 minutes.
Add low-sodium fish sauce. Toss 20 seconds. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 228.8mg, Na 89.3mg, Phos 33.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Shrimp Paste Rice
recipes.description: Rice with shrimp paste and vegetables
instructions: Cook prawns: heat 5 ml rice bran oil in a wok. Add prawns and stir-fry 2–3 minutes until pink. Remove.
Add remaining oil. Stir-fry minced shallot and garlic 2 minutes until soft.
Add cooked cold white rice. Stir-fry 2 minutes, pressing against the wok.
Return prawns. Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds.
Scatter fresh coriander and sliced spring onion. Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 230.7mg, Na 64.5mg, Phos 173.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Gaeng Som
recipes.description: Sour curry with seafood
instructions: Heat rice bran oil in a pot over medium heat. Add minced shallot and garlic. Cook 3 minutes until softened.
Add white fish cubes. Cook 2 minutes without stirring.
Add diced green papaya and tomato. Add 500 ml water. Bring to a simmer.
Add low-sodium fish sauce. Simmer gently 15–20 minutes until fish is cooked and papaya is tender.
Add lime juice at the end and stir. Taste and adjust sourness.
Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 400.1mg, Na 61.1mg, Phos 137.2mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Larb Moo
recipes.description: Spicy pork salad
instructions: Cook ground pork in a dry pan over medium heat, breaking into fine pieces, until cooked through. Cool slightly.
In a bowl, combine cooked pork with sliced shallot, fresh mint, coriander, and minced lemongrass.
Whisk together lime juice, honey, and low-sodium fish sauce for the dressing.
Pour dressing over the pork mixture and toss well.
Taste — the salad should be sour, a little sweet, and lightly savoury.
Serve immediately at room temperature.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 484.3mg, Na 93.2mg, Phos 219.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad Grapow Gai
recipes.description: Chicken with Thai holy basil
instructions: Heat rice bran oil in a wok over high heat until smoking.
Add minced garlic and chilli, stir-fry 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add ground chicken, break into very fine pieces, and stir-fry 5–6 minutes until fully cooked with no pink.
Add diced red bell pepper, stir-fry 2 minutes.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice, toss 30 seconds.

recipes.name: Thai Ceviche

Pour dressing over and toss gently.

recipes.name: Som Tam Shrimp

Add lime juice. Whisk once more.
Heat rice bran oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Pour in egg white mixture. Cook undisturbed 1.5 minutes until edges are set.
Fold in half and slide onto a plate. Serve immediately with sliced cucumber.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 229.1mg, Na 154.4mg, Phos 23.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Squid Salad with Lime
recipes.description: Spicy Thai squid salad with lime dressing — squid is lower in phosphorus than many seafoods.
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a rolling boil. Add squid and blanch exactly 1.5–2 minutes. Drain. Cool under cold water.
Slice cucumber. Thinly slice shallot.
Whisk lime juice, honey, chilli, and a pinch of black pepper for the dressing.
Combine squid, cucumber, shallot, fresh mint, and coriander in a bowl.
Pour dressing over. Toss well.
Serve immediately or chill briefly.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 349.7mg, Na 38.9mg, Phos 189.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Pumpkin Soup with Lemongrass
recipes.description: Thai-spiced pumpkin soup — kabocha pumpkin with lemongrass and galangal.
instructions: Peel and cube kabocha pumpkin.
Heat rice bran oil in a pot. Sauté diced shallot and garlic 3 minutes.
Add pumpkin cubes. Stir 2 minutes to coat.
Add 700 ml water, bruised lemongrass, and sliced galangal. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer 20 minutes until pumpkin is very tender.
Remove and discard lemongrass and galangal.
Blend until completely smooth.
Add lime juice. Season with white pepper.
Serve topped with fresh coriander.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 389.5mg, Na 7.3mg, Phos 58.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Grilled Prawn with Lime
recipes.description: Simply grilled prawns with a Thai lime-herb dipping sauce — minimal seasoning.
instructions: Whisk garlic, finely minced lemongrass, lime juice, honey, and chilli together as a marinade.
Toss prawns in marinade. Marinate 10 minutes.
Heat grill or grill pan to medium-high.
Grill prawns 2–3 minutes per side until pink with slight char marks.
Scatter fresh coriander. Serve immediately with extra lime.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 272.1mg, Na 86.2mg, Phos 189.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Larb Gai (Chicken Herb Salad)
recipes.description: Thai minced chicken herb salad — adapted with lime dressing instead of full fish sauce.
instructions: Cook ground chicken in a dry pan over medium heat, breaking into fine pieces, 5–6 minutes. Cool slightly.
Combine cooked chicken with sliced shallot, fresh mint, coriander, and minced lemongrass.
Whisk lime juice, honey, low-sodium fish sauce, and chilli together.
Pour dressing over chicken mixture. Toss well.
If using, scatter toasted ground rice powder over the top for texture.
Serve immediately at room temperature.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 381.7mg, Na 87.8mg, Phos 219.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Green Curry with Vegetables (No Coconut)
recipes.description: Green curry with water base — all the aromatics without coconut milk potassium.
instructions: Heat rice bran oil in a wok over medium heat. Add green curry paste and stir-fry 1 minute until fragrant.
Add sliced chicken breast. Toss to coat. Cook 3 minutes.
Add sliced zucchini and green beans.
Pour in 250 ml water. Add lemongrass and kaffir lime leaves.
Bring to a simmer. Add low-sodium fish sauce. Simmer 10 minutes until chicken is cooked.
Stir in lime juice and fresh Thai basil.
Remove aromatics. Serve over white rice.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 501.4mg, Na 122.7mg, Phos 214.6mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Stir-Fried Cabbage with Fish Sauce
recipes.description: Thai-style garlic cabbage — lightly seasoned with minimal fish sauce.
instructions: Heat a wok over high heat until smoking. Add rice bran oil.
Add sliced garlic and chilli. Stir-fry 20 seconds until garlic turns golden.
Add shredded napa cabbage. Toss vigorously over maximum heat 3–4 minutes.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds.
Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: The low-sodium preparation preserves nutrients for kidney health. (Nutrition per serving: K 331.8mg, Na 27.3mg, Phos 46.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Crispy Egg White Omelette (Khai Jiao)
recipes.description: Thai-style crispy egg white omelette — pan-fried in minimal oil with fish sauce and lime.
instructions: Whisk egg whites with low-sodium fish sauce, lime juice, and white pepper vigorously until slightly frothy.
Heat rice bran oil in a non-stick skillet over high heat until almost smoking.
Pour in egg whites all at once. Cook undisturbed 90 seconds until edges are crispy and golden.
Fold in thirds. Slide onto plate.
Top with sliced spring onion and fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 262.6mg, Na 228.2mg, Phos 27.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Cold Rice Noodle Salad
recipes.description: Room temperature rice noodle salad with Thai herbs and lime — no cooking required.
instructions: Cook rice vermicelli in unsalted water until soft. Drain. Rinse under cold water. Cool completely.
Blanch carrot julienne 1 minute in boiling water. Cool.
Julienne cucumber. Slice spring onion.
Whisk lime juice, honey, and a pinch of chilli flakes together.
Combine noodles, cucumber, carrot, and spring onion in a bowl.
Pour dressing over and toss well.
Fold in fresh mint and coriander. Serve at room temperature.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 224.4mg, Na 97.2mg, Phos 54.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Chicken Congee with Lemongrass
recipes.description: Thai-spiced rice congee — fragrant with lemongrass and galangal.
instructions: Wash rice. Combine with 1000 ml water, lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves in a large pot.
Bring to a boil. Reduce to lowest simmer.
Cook uncovered 40 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes, until creamy porridge forms.
Add sliced or shredded chicken breast. Simmer 8 more minutes.
Remove and discard lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Season with white pepper.
Ladle into bowls. Top with spring onion and fresh coriander. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 196.5mg, Na 37.8mg, Phos 121.3mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Pad Pak (Thai Stir-Fried Vegetables)
recipes.description: Thai stir-fried vegetables with garlic and light fish sauce seasoning.
instructions: Blanch bok choy in boiling water 1.5 minutes. Drain.
Trim snow peas.
Heat wok over high heat. Add rice bran oil.
Add sliced garlic and ginger. Stir-fry 20 seconds.
Add snow peas. Stir-fry 1.5 minutes.
Add bean sprouts and blanched bok choy. Stir-fry 1.5 minutes.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice. Toss 30 seconds.
Season with white pepper. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 305.2mg, Na 52.8mg, Phos 72.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Herb Steamed Fish
recipes.description: Fish steamed with full array of Thai aromatics — the most aromatic renal-safe fish preparation.
instructions: Arrange lemongrass, galangal, and kaffir lime leaves on a heatproof plate as an aromatic bed.
Pat fish fillets dry and place on top.
Drizzle low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice evenly over the fish.
Bring steamer water to a vigorous boil. Place plate in steamer. Cover tightly.
Steam 10–12 minutes until fish is opaque and flakes easily.
Remove from steamer. Drain accumulated liquid.
Heat rice bran oil until smoking. Pour over fresh coriander scattered on the fish.
Serve in the steaming plate immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 476.3mg, Na 88.3mg, Phos 208.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Cucumber Relish (Ajad)
recipes.description: Classic ajad (Thai cucumber relish) — served as a side with grilled dishes.
instructions: Dissolve honey in rice vinegar and 20 ml water in a small saucepan over low heat, about 1 minute. Cool.
Slice cucumber thinly into rounds. Thinly slice shallot into rings.
Combine cucumber and shallot in a bowl.
Pour cooled dressing over. Add sliced chilli and a pinch of white pepper.
Toss gently. Refrigerate 15 minutes before serving.
Serve cold as a side condiment alongside grilled dishes.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 104.9mg, Na 2.3mg, Phos 17.8mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Steamed Egg with Minced Pork
recipes.description: Thai steamed egg custard with a pork filling — smooth silken texture.
instructions: Cook ground pork with finely minced lemongrass in a small pan over medium heat, breaking into fine pieces, until cooked through. Season with low-sodium fish sauce. Cool.
Whisk egg whites and 1 whole egg with 150 ml cooled boiled water and a pinch of white pepper.
Strain mixture through a fine sieve into 2 heatproof bowls.
Place cooked pork in the base of each bowl. Pour egg mixture over.
Cover each bowl tightly with foil.
Steam over low heat 12–15 minutes until just set. Do not use high heat — it causes bubbles.
Scatter fresh coriander. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: This dish is low in sodium and potassium, making it ideal for renal patients. (Nutrition per serving: K 260.8mg, Na 149.2mg, Phos 132.9mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Green Mango Chicken Salad
recipes.description: Light Thai-style salad with green mango — green mango is much lower in sugar and potassium.
instructions: Poach chicken breast in simmering water 18–20 minutes. Cool and shred.
Shred green mango with a julienne peeler or box grater.
Thinly slice shallot.
Whisk lime juice, honey, and a pinch of chilli for the dressing.
Combine shredded chicken, green mango, and shallot.
Pour dressing over. Toss gently.
Fold in fresh mint and coriander last. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 349.2mg, Na 60.9mg, Phos 182.4mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Galangal Chicken Soup (Clear)
recipes.description: Clear chicken soup with galangal and lemongrass — lighter than tom kha, equally aromatic.
instructions: Bring 700 ml water to a boil with galangal, lemongrass, and kaffir lime leaves. Simmer 5 minutes.
Add sliced button mushrooms. Simmer 4 minutes.
Add chicken breast pieces. Reduce to a gentle simmer. Cook 15 minutes until cooked through.
Remove and discard all aromatics.
Add low-sodium fish sauce and lime juice.
Scatter fresh coriander. Serve hot.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 270.9mg, Na 53.8mg, Phos 136.5mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Herb Omelette with Fish Sauce
recipes.description: Thai-style egg white omelette — crispy edges, aromatic with Thai herbs.
instructions: Whisk egg whites with low-sodium fish sauce, lime juice, and white pepper vigorously until slightly frothy.
Heat rice bran oil in a non-stick skillet over high heat until almost smoking.
Pour in egg whites all at once. Cook undisturbed 90 seconds until edges are crispy and golden.
Fold in thirds. Slide onto plate.
Top with fresh coriander and sliced spring onion. Serve immediately.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 278.2mg, Na 229.6mg, Phos 29.0mg. USDA-verified values.)

recipes.name: Thai Steamed Tofu with Lemongrass Sauce
recipes.description: Cold silken tofu with a Thai lemongrass-lime sauce — a refreshing no-cook dish.
instructions: Refrigerate silken tofu at least 1 hour before serving.

Make lemongrass-lime sauce: combine finely minced lemongrass, lime juice, honey, minced garlic, fresh coriander, and chilli. Mix well. Let sit 5 minutes so lemongrass softens in the acidic lime juice.
Carefully unmould chilled tofu onto a shallow serving plate.
Slice gently into 1.5 cm slabs with clean downward cuts — do not drag the knife.
Pour lemongrass-lime sauce generously over the tofu.
Scatter additional coriander on top. Serve immediately, cold.
recipes.renalNote: Use low-sodium seasoning and measured portions to support renal dietary targets. (Nutrition per serving: K 304.6mg, Na 70.8mg, Phos 157.2mg. USDA-verified values.)
